blocks:
Ayahs

تَنزِيلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ (۱)
The [gradual] sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise. (1)
إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ فَٱعْبُدِ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصًا لَّهُ ٱلدِّينَ (۲)
Indeed We have sent down the Book to you with the truth; so worship Allah, putting exclusive faith in Him. (2)

Key Points

"Tanzīl" means gradual descent, and "Inzāl" means sudden and immediate descent. In these verses, both types of the Qur'an's revelation are mentioned. The word "Izzah" (might/honor) appears 88 times in the Qur'an, 46 of which are alongside wisdom (Hikmah). "Al-'Azīz Al-Hakīm" (The All-Mighty, The All-Wise). Yes, might is permanent only when accompanied by wisdom. The truly Mighty One is only Him, because He is the Creator and the Powerful; He knows the unseen and the manifest, and the expansion and restriction of provision is in His hand. We read in a Hadith: Whoever desires the might of this world and the Hereafter must obey the Mighty God. "Faman arāda ‘izz ad-dārayn falyuṭi‘ al-‘Azīz" (1) The repetition of the word Kitāb (Book) in two consecutive verses—where once He says, "The Book is from the Mighty God," and the next time He says, "The Book is the truth"—the secret is that the path to might for the individual and society is connection to the Mighty God and the school of truth. Obedience to anyone other than God and following any religion other than the school of revelation will have a humiliating beginning or end. Apart from the Mighty God, everyone is limited and perishable, and except for the school of revelation, all other schools of thought are either narrow-minded, superstitious, strict, or unnatural. Yes, a school of thought created by dependent humans cannot build a mighty society, and thus, in the third verse, He says: Pure religion belongs exclusively to God. Although the address regarding sincere worship is directed at the Prophet, in reality, this address includes all people. Linguistically, "Dīn" is used to mean recompense, way of life, nation, worship, and obedience (2), but here it refers to worship, because the discussion is about worship. "Fa‘budillāha mukhliṣan lahu ad-dīn" (So worship Allah, making the religion sincerely for Him).

1) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayān. 2) Qāmūs al-Muḥīṭ.

Messages

1- We must appreciate the heavenly Book, because it has been revealed by the Wise God and is a manifestation of His Might and Wisdom. "Min Allāh Al-‘Azīz Al-Hakīm"

2- Contrary to the might of tyrants, which is accompanied by despotism, Divine Might is accompanied by wisdom. "Al-‘Azīz Al-Hakīm"

3- Since God is Mighty and invincible, His word is also victorious and invincible against all schools of thought and statements. "Al-Kitāb min Allāh Al-‘Azīz Al-Hakīm"

4- The Qur'an grants might and teaches wisdom. "Al-Kitāb min Allāh Al-‘Azīz Al-Hakīm"

5- Although the Qur'an was revealed gradually over 23 years, "Tanzīl al-Kitāb," it was revealed suddenly and completely upon the blessed heart of the Prophet at the beginning of the mission. "Innā anzalnā"

6- The Qur'an, like God, is truth and constant. "Bil-Ḥaqq" (The Qur'an is entirely truth and far removed from any speculation or false statement). "Bil-Ḥaqq"

7- The reason and philosophy behind the revelation of the Qur'an is to present the truth to the people. "Tanzīl al-Kitāb... bil-Ḥaqq"

8- Worship must be based on truth. "Bil-Ḥaqq fa‘bud" (The path to felicity is centering oneself on the school of thought, worship, and sincerity).

9- Just as the goal of creation and existence is worship, "Wa mā khalaqtu al-jinna wa al-insa illā liya‘budūn" (3), the goal of legislation (Sharī‘ah) and the revelation of the Book is also worship. "Innā anzalnā al-Kitāb... fa‘budillāh"

10- The revelation of the Book is a blessing that deserves Divine gratitude. "Tanzīl al-Kitāb... fa‘bud"

11- Religious practice must be far removed from desires, fantasies, and superstitions. "Mukhliṣan lahu ad-dīn"

12- In acts of worship, the intention of seeking closeness (to Allah) is necessary. "Fa‘budillāh mukhliṣan" (3) Adh-Dhāriyāt, 56.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Az-Zumar: This Surah has seventy-five verses and was revealed in Mecca. Like other Meccan Surahs, most of its content concerns God and the Day of Judgment. "Zumar" means groups or crowds, and the naming of this Surah with this word is because the inhabitants of Hell and the inhabitants of Paradise will enter Hell or Paradise in groups, as mentioned in verses 71 and 73. Monotheism (Tawḥīd) in creation, Lordship (Rubūbiyyah), and worship, especially sincerity (Ikhlāṣ) in worship and servitude, has been given the most attention and emphasis in this Surah. The presence of people in the court of the Day of Judgment, God's judgment based on the record of deeds and the testimony of witnesses, and the driving of sinners to Hell and the pure to Paradise, are among the topics detailed in this Surah.

Ayah

أَلَا لِلَّهِ ٱلدِّينُ ٱلْخَالِصُ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ مَا نَعْبُدُهُمْ إِلَّا لِيُقَرِّبُونَآ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ زُلْفَىٰٓ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ فِى مَا هُمْ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى مَنْ هُوَ كَـٰذِبٌ كَفَّارٌ (۳)
Indeed, only exclusive faith is worthy of Allah, and those who take other as awliya besides Him [claiming,] ‘We only worship them so that they may bring us near to Allah,’ Allah will judge between them concerning that about which they differ. Indeed Allah does not guide someone who is a liar and an ingrate. (3)

[Exception in en chunk 1]

Ayah

لَّوْ أَرَادَ ٱللَّهُ أَن يَتَّخِذَ وَلَدًا لَّٱصْطَفَىٰ مِمَّا يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ ۖ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْوَٰحِدُ ٱلْقَهَّارُ (٤)
Had Allah intended to take a son, He could have chosen from those He has created whatever He wished. Immaculate is He! He is Allah, the One, the All-paramount. (4)

Messages

1- Allah has neither a real son (as stated in) «لم یلد و لم یولد», nor an adopted son. «لو اراد اللّه ان یتخذ ولداً»

2- If Allah desired a son, He would choose from among the best of creation, not from stone and wood. «ان یتّخذ ولداً لاصطفى مما یخلق»

3- The desire for a child is a sign of need, and He is exalted (above this). «سبحانه »

4- If the son were real, it would be a sign that God is physical, divisible, has a likeness, and has a spouse, whereas He is One. He is not divisible and has no spouse or likeness. «و هو الواحد»

5- If the son were chosen (adopted), taking a son would be a sign of physical need or spiritual companionship, whereas He is the All-Dominant (Al-Qahhar). «و هو القهار»

Ayah

خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ يُكَوِّرُ ٱلَّيْلَ عَلَى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُكَوِّرُ ٱلنَّهَارَ عَلَى ٱلَّيْلِ ۖ وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ ۖ كُلٌّ يَجْرِى لِأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۗ أَلَا هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْغَفَّـٰرُ (٥)
He created the heavens and the earth with consummate wisdom. He winds the night over the day, and winds the day over the night, and He has disposed the sun and the moon, each moving for a specified term. Indeed, He is the All-mighty, the All-forgiver! (5)

Messages

1- The creation of existence is not a random affair. «خلق السموات»

2- Both the system of Divine Law (Tashrī') is based on truth «انا انزلنا الیك كتاب بالحقّ»(6) and the system of creation (Takwīn) is based on truth. «خلق السموات و الارض بالحقّ»

3- He is both the Creator «خَلَق» and the Administrator (Mudabbir). «یكوّر»

4- The sun and the moon are constantly in motion. «كلٌ یجرى»

5- The movements of the celestial bodies are time-bound. «لاجلٍ»

6- The timing of the movements is calculated and specified. «مسّمى »

7- The previous Ayah concluded with the word «قهار» (The Dominant) and this Ayah concludes with the word «غفّار» (The Forgiving), so that fear and hope may exist side by side.

8- Allah's pardon is not based on inability or weakness; rather, He forgives despite His power. «العزیز الغفار»

9- The creation of the heavens and the earth, the movements of the moon and the sun, and the wrapping of night around day are manifestations of Divine Might. «خَلَق، سَخَّر، یكّورُ، هو العزیز» (6) Zumar, 3.

Ayah

خَلَقَكُم مِّن نَّفْسٍ وَٰحِدَةٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنْهَا زَوْجَهَا وَأَنزَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ ثَمَـٰنِيَةَ أَزْوَٰجٍ ۚ يَخْلُقُكُمْ فِى بُطُونِ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ خَلْقًا مِّنۢ بَعْدِ خَلْقٍ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتٍ ثَلَـٰثٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ ۖ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُصْرَفُونَ (٦)
He created you from a single soul, then made from it its mate, and He has sent down for you eight mates of the cattle. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers, creation after creation, in a threefold darkness. That is Allah, your Lord! To Him belongs all sovereignty. There is no god except Him. Then where are you being led away? (6)

Key Points

The meaning of "ثمانیة ازواج" (eight pairs) here, based on Ayahs 143 and 144 of Surah Al-An'am, which state: "من الضأن اثنین و من المَعز اثنین" (two of the sheep and two of the goats), "و من الابل اثنین و من البقر اثنین" (and two of the camels and two of the cattle), refers to the male and female of four types of animals: sheep, goat, camel, and cow. The meaning of "أنزَل" (He sent down) is not a physical descent, but rather a blessing bestowed from a higher station upon a lower station. Or it means provision, similar to the Ayah "نُزُلاً من عنداللّه" (a provision from Allah) (7). Or it is because the source of life for animals is water that descends from above, and the treasury of all blessings is above. "ان من شى ء الا عندنا خزائنه و ما ننزّله الاّ بقدر معلوم" (There is not a thing but with Us are its treasures, and We do not send it down except in a known measure) (8). There is nothing except that its treasury is with Us, and We do not send it down except in a known measure. The meaning of the "three darknesses" (ظلمات سه گانه), according to Imam Husayn عليه السلام in the Du'a of 'Arafah, is flesh, skin, and blood. "ثمّ اسكنتَنى فى ظلمات ثلاث بین لحم و جلد و دم" (Then You settled me in three darknesses: between flesh, skin, and blood).

7) Al 'Imran, 198. 8) Al-Hijr, 21.

Messages

1- The origin of all human beings, male and female, white and black, is one. "نفس واحدة" (a single soul).

2- Although men and women have physical differences, their spirit is one. "نفس واحدة" (a single soul).

3- Spousal relationship (Zaujiyyah) is a blessing, in addition to creation itself, that Allah has bestowed upon humanity. "خلقكم... ثمّ جعل... زوجها" (He created you... then He made... his spouse).

4- Man needs a spouse for the survival of the species. "خلقكم... ثمّ جعل منها زوجها" (He created you... then He made from it his spouse).

5- Stomach (hunger) and desire (lust) are the primary needs of man, which Allah has addressed. "جعل منها زوجها و انزل... الانعام" (He made from it his spouse and sent down... the cattle).

6- Among the types of animals, four animals (sheep, goat, cow, and camel) play a special role in human sustenance. "ثمانیة ازواج" (eight pairs).

7- The creation of man takes place in several stages. "خلقاً من بعد خلق" (creation after creation). (In Ayahs 12 and 13 of Surah Al-Mu'minun, these stages are referred to as "نطفة, علقة, مضغة" [a drop of seed, a clot, a lump of flesh]).

8- Man is paralyzed in darkness, but Allah is active in accumulated darknesses. "یخلقكم فى ظلمات ثلاث" (He creates you in three darknesses).

9- We should recognize the blessings of life as coming from Allah, for He is the Creator and the Owner, the Lord (Rabb) and the Worshipped One (Ma'bud). "خلقكم... ربكم له الملك لا اله الاّ هو" (He created you... your Lord; to Him belongs the dominion. There is no god but He).

Ayah

إِن تَكْفُرُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ عَنكُمْ ۖ وَلَا يَرْضَىٰ لِعِبَادِهِ ٱلْكُفْرَ ۖ وَإِن تَشْكُرُوا۟ يَرْضَهُ لَكُمْ ۗ وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۗ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُم مَّرْجِعُكُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (۷)
If you are ungrateful, Allah has indeed no need of you, though He does not approve ingratitude for His servants; and if you give thanks, He approves that for you. No bearer shall bear another’s burden; then your return will be to your Lord, whereat He will inform you concerning what you used to do. Indeed, He knows best what is in the breasts. (7)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, Allah's blessings in the creation of man and his material sustenance were discussed. In this Ayah, the discussion is about man's duty to be grateful for these blessings. The meaning of kufr (disbelief/ingratitude) in this Ayah is ingratitude for blessings (kufrān-i ni'mat), because gratitude (tashakkur) is mentioned in opposition to it. Allah loves us more than we love ourselves. He does not approve of ingratitude and unthankfulness for us, but we approve it for ourselves. Since the Ayah states that everyone is responsible for his own deeds and the place for accountability is the Day of Judgment, the Ayah concludes with the phrase «انّه علیم بذات الصدور» (Indeed, He is Knower of what is in the breasts) to state that the Judge of that Day knows all things.

Messages

1- The command to worship is not a sign of Allah's need for us. «ان تكفروا فان اللّه غنىٌّ»

(Just as the instruction to build a house facing the sun is not a sign of the sun's need for us. Allah is in need of nothing, not only from us but from anything, as He says elsewhere: «فانّ اللّه غنىٌ عن العالمین»(9)).

2- Do not attribute your disbelief (kufr) and deviation to the Divine will and decree. (Some disbelievers and polytheists considered their path to be the will of Allah). «لا یرضى لعباده الكفر»

3- Allah considers even the deviants to be His servants. «لعباده الكفر»

4- Gratitude is the key to Allah's satisfaction. «ان تشكروا یرضه لكم»

5- In the method of invitation (Da'wah), we should proceed step-by-step. The first step: He said He is Self-Sufficient. The second step: He said He is not pleased with your disbelief. The third step: He said your faith and gratitude are for your own benefit. And finally, He said: If you rebel, you will be held accountable. «غنى عنكم - لا یرضى - ینبّئكم»

6- Allah is Just, and everyone sees the recompense for his own deeds. «لا تزر وازرةٌ»

7- Everyone is responsible for his own deeds, and the sin of one person cannot be placed upon another. «و لا تزر وازرة وزر اخرى»

8- When threatening those whose sin is not yet certain, leave the door open for forgiveness. (The word «ینبئكم» says: He will inform you of your deeds, and does not say: He will punish you for your deeds).

9- The amount of reward and punishment depends on our own actions. «ما كنتم تعملون»

10- Precision in reward and punishment is related to Allah's exact knowledge. «انّه علیم بذات الصدور»

11- Divine knowledge is both deep and extensive, covering both the apparent and the hidden. «بذات الصدور»

12- Allah knows the intentions and motivations behind deeds. «علیم بذات الصدور» (9) Āl ‘Imrān, 97.

Ayah

۞ وَإِذَا مَسَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ ضُرٌّ دَعَا رَبَّهُۥ مُنِيبًا إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ إِذَا خَوَّلَهُۥ نِعْمَةً مِّنْهُ نَسِىَ مَا كَانَ يَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ مِن قَبْلُ وَجَعَلَ لِلَّهِ أَندَادًا لِّيُضِلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ ۚ قُلْ تَمَتَّعْ بِكُفْرِكَ قَلِيلًا ۖ إِنَّكَ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلنَّارِ (۸)
When distress befalls man, he supplicates his Lord, turning to Him penitently. Then, when He grants him a blessing from Himself, he forgets that for which he had supplicated Him earlier and sets up equals to Allah, that he may lead [people] astray from His way. Say, ‘Revel in your ingratitude for a while. Indeed you are among the inmates of the Fire.’ (8)

Key Points

«ضُرّ» means any kind of harm or damage. «خَوَّله» means a great bestowal. «منیب» is either derived from «نوبة» (nawbah), meaning that man turns to God intermittently (turn by turn), or it is derived from «نابَ» (nāba), meaning complete severance (inqitā‘), meaning that man turns completely to Him, and apparently, this second meaning is intended. In the previous verses, the recognition of God was discussed through creation, while in this verse, attention to God is discussed through innate nature (fitrah) and the inner self.

Messages

1- The Quran criticizes the seasonal and situational attentions of man, which arise during distress and are later forgotten. «اذا مسّ الانسان ضُرٌّ دَعا»

2- Man has little capacity (is impatient) and cries out at the first encounter with hardship. «مسّ»

3- Difficulties are a factor for awakening and returning to the innate nature (fitrah). «ضُرٌّ دَعا» (Afflictions compel man toward repentance and supplication).

4- Sincere supplication causes the removal of misfortunes. «منیباً الیه... خَوّله نعمةً»

5- Harms are not from Him, but blessings are from Him. «نعمةً منه»

6- Prosperity leads to heedlessness and forgetfulness. «نعمةً - نسى»

7- Impatient people forget their previous difficulties when they achieve prosperity. «نسى ما كان یدعوا الیه»

8- Heedlessness of the remembrance of Allah leads to polytheism (shirk), and whoever forgets Allah due to prosperity turns to others besides Him. «نسى... جعل للّه انداداً»

9- Man's polytheism is extensive, and he attaches his heart to something new every day. «انداداً»

10- Man's attention to others besides Allah also leads others into error and misguidance. «لیضلّ»

11- Man's deviation is step-by-step. First: Forgetfulness («نسى»). Second: Polytheism («جعل للّه انداداً»). Third: Misguiding others («لیضلّ»).

12- Not every enjoyment is a sign of favor. «تمتع بكفرك»

13- The polytheist is an unbeliever (kāfir). «جَعَل للّه انداداً... تمتّع بكفرك»

14- Worldly enjoyments are little compared to the Hereafter. «تمتّع... قلیلاً»

15- The punishment for ingratitude (kufrān) and disbelief (kufr) is being joined with the inhabitants of the Fire. «انك من اصحاب النار»

Ayah

أَمَّنْ هُوَ قَـٰنِتٌ ءَانَآءَ ٱلَّيْلِ سَاجِدًا وَقَآئِمًا يَحْذَرُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ وَيَرْجُوا۟ رَحْمَةَ رَبِّهِۦ ۗ قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِى ٱلَّذِينَ يَعْلَمُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ ۗ إِنَّمَا يَتَذَكَّرُ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (۹)
Is he who supplicates in the watches of the night, prostrating and standing, apprehensive of the Hereafter and expecting the mercy of his Lord...? Say, ‘Are those who know equal to those who do not know?’ Only those who possess intellect take admonition. (9)

Key Points

Alongside the previous Ayah, which described the characteristics of the dwellers of the Fire, this Ayah describes the characteristics of the believers. In the previous Ayah, He said: A group pays attention to Allah only during calamity and forgets Him during prosperity, but in this Ayah, He says: Believers are always mindful of God, whether in prosperity or hardship. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of qunūt (devotion) at night, mentioned in "قانت آناء اللیل" (devout during the hours of the night), is standing up for the night prayer (Salat al-Layl). (10)

10) Kāfī, Vol. 3, p. 444.

Messages

1- Night is the best setting for worship. «قانتٌ آناءَ اللَّیل»

2- The sign of knowledge is servitude (to Allah). «ساجداً و قائماً... الّذین یعلمون» (Knowledge and worship must go together. Indeed, the night-vigil keepers who worship and the seekers of the Hereafter who are hopeful of Divine mercy are the true scholars. «قانتٌ آناءَ اللَّیل یحذر الآخرة... یعلمون»)

3- Worship is valuable when it is continuous. («ساجداً و قائماً» is presented in the form of an active participle, which indicates continuity).

4- Men of God both fear the Hereafter and hope for Divine mercy. «یحذر, یرجوا»

5- Our fear is due to our own deeds «یحذر الآخرة», but our hope is in His grace and mercy. «یرجوا رحمة ربّه»

6- One of the methods of education is comparing good deeds and bad deeds, and the good people and the bad people. «أمن هو قانت», «هل یستوى الّذین یعلمون و الّذین لا یعلمون»

7- Accepting advice is a sign of sound intellect. «انما یتذكّر اولوا الالباب»

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰعِبَادِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ۚ لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا۟ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةٌ ۗ وَأَرْضُ ٱللَّهِ وَٰسِعَةٌ ۗ إِنَّمَا يُوَفَّى ٱلصَّـٰبِرُونَ أَجْرَهُم بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ (۱٠)
Say, ‘[Allah declares:] “O My servants who have faith! Be wary of your Lord. For those who do good in this world there will be a good [reward], and Allah’s earth is vast. Indeed the patient will be paid in full their reward without any reckoning.” ’ (10)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: For a group of people, no file will be opened on the Day of Resurrection, and they will enter Paradise without questioning or reckoning. Then the Prophet recited this Ayah: «انّما یوفّى الصابرون اجرهم بغیر حساب» (11)

(11) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- When inviting others to good deeds, grant dignity to the addressee. «یا عباد»

2- Faith alone is not sufficient; it must be accompanied by piety (taqwa) and avoidance of sin. «آمنوا - اتّقوا»

3- A good deed is valuable only if it originates from the people of faith and piety. «آمنوا اتّقوا... احسنوا»

4- Allah reciprocates good deeds in kind. «للّذین احسنوا... حسنة» (Just as He says in other verses: «اِن احسنتم احسنتم لانفسكم» (12) If you do good, you do good to yourselves. «هل جزاء الاحسان الاّ الاحسان» (13) Is the reward of goodness anything but goodness?)

5- Sometimes maintaining piety necessitates migration (Hijrah). «آمنوا اتّقوا... ارض اللّه واسعة»

6- Migration is the prelude to receiving Divine favors. «ارض اللّه واسعة... اجرهم بغیر حساب»

7- Migration requires patience (sabr) and steadfastness. «ارض اللّه واسعة انّما یوفّى الصابرون»

(12) Isra, 7.

(13) Ar-Rahman, 60.

Ayahs

قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصًا لَّهُ ٱلدِّينَ (۱۱)
Say, ‘I have been commanded to worship Allah with exclusive faith in Him, (11)
وَأُمِرْتُ لِأَنْ أَكُونَ أَوَّلَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ (۱۲)
and I have been commanded to be the foremost of those who submit [to Him].’ (12)
قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّى عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (۱۳)
Say, ‘Indeed, should I disobey my Lord, I fear the punishment of a tremendous day.’ (13)

Messages

1- By declaring God-centeredness, make the polytheists despair of the hope of infiltrating your thought and school of thought. «قل انّى اُمرتُ»

2- The Prophet does not say or do anything on his own accord. «اُمرتُ... اُمرتُ»

3- The leader must be foremost in perfections. «اوّل المسلمین»

4- The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله acted single-handedly in performing sincere worship and his other duties, whether others cooperated or opposed him. «اُمرت - مخلصاً - اول المسلمین»

5- In the Court of Divine Justice, the Prophets are no different from others. «اِن عصیتُ ربّى عذاب یوم عظیم»

6- Faith in the punishment of the Resurrection is the strongest deterrent factor against sin. «انّى أخاف ِان عصیتُ ربّى عذاب یوم عظیم»

Ayahs

قُلِ ٱللَّهَ أَعْبُدُ مُخْلِصًا لَّهُۥ دِينِى (۱٤)
Say, ‘I worship [only] Allah, putting my exclusive faith in Him. (14)
فَٱعْبُدُوا۟ مَا شِئْتُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَأَهْلِيهِمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ أَلَا ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْخُسْرَانُ ٱلْمُبِينُ (۱٥)
You worship whatever you wish besides Him.’ Say, ‘The losers are those who ruin themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection.’ Indeed, that is a manifest loss! (15)

Key Points

"Loss" (خسارت) is the forfeiture of the principal capital. A seller of ice who has no customers not only fails to profit but also sees his principal capital melt away. The loss of the self (خُسرانِ نفس) means its destruction, such that its potentials are lost. The word "loss" (خسارت) is repeated three times in this Ayah to awaken man regarding the loss of the self on the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). Turning towards anything other than Allah, whatever it may be and whoever it may be, is self-betrayal and loss. In a transaction, man should either profit, or at least not incur a loss, or at least incur a small loss. But the polytheists lose the Hereafter, and thus their loss is manifest and clear. Furthermore, the loss of the self in the Hereafter is irreparable.

Messages

1- Clearly declare your stance against the disbelievers. «قل»

2- Monotheism is the most prominent message of the Prophets. «قل اللّه اعبد»

3- The Prophets executed Divine commands wholeheartedly. (In the preceding Ayahs, we read: «إنّى اُمرتُ ان اعبد اللّه مخلصاً» [Indeed, I have been commanded to worship Allah, being sincere to Him in obedience], and in this Ayah, He says: «قل اللّه اعبد مخلصاً» [Say: I worship Allah, being sincere to Him].)

4- Since the beginning of this Surah, sincerity (اخلاص) has been mentioned 4 times, and this is because throughout history, the greatest affliction for religion has been the addition of superstitions, personal preferences, and distortions. «مخلصاً له دینى»

5- In implementing religious laws, we must refrain from accepting any change or transformation and from submitting to the preferences of others. «مخلصاً له دینى»

6- In the method of propagation, sometimes threat and severity are necessary. «فاعبدوا ما شئتم» (So worship what you will) (Just as He says elsewhere: «اعملوا ما شئتم»(14) [Do whatever you will].)

7- The leader must know that not all people will submit to him. «فاعبدوا ما شئتم»

8- Once we separate from Allah, it makes no difference what we connect ourselves to. «ما شئتم»

9- Self-forfeiture is the greatest of losses. «انّ الخاسرین الّذین خسروا انفسهم»

10- Man is responsible for his family. «و اهلیهم»

11- The real profit and loss occur on the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). «یوم القیامة»

12- For those who are slow to believe, we should repeat the truth. «اَلا ذلك هو الخسران...» (14) Fussilat, 40.

Ayah

لَهُم مِّن فَوْقِهِمْ ظُلَلٌ مِّنَ ٱلنَّارِ وَمِن تَحْتِهِمْ ظُلَلٌ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يُخَوِّفُ ٱللَّهُ بِهِۦ عِبَادَهُۥ ۚ يَـٰعِبَادِ فَٱتَّقُونِ (۱٦)
There will be canopies of fire above them, and [similar] canopies beneath them. With that Allah deters His servants. So, My servants, be wary of Me! (16)

Messages

1- The punishment for those who abandoned Allah and went after others besides Him and suffered loss, is the encompassing fire from above, below, and every direction. «من فوقهم ظلل من النار...»

2- Polytheism (shirk) in this world results in wrath in the Hereafter. «من فوقهم ظلل من النار»

3- Hell is full of fire and darkness. «ظلل من النّار...»

4- Divine warnings are based on His mercy. «یا عباد فاتّقون»

5- Piety (taqwa) is the cause of deliverance from the fire of Hell. «ظلل من النار... فاتّقون»

6- Belief in the dangers of the Resurrection (Qiyāmah) is the cause of piety (taqwa). «ظلل من النار... فاتّقون»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱجْتَنَبُوا۟ ٱلطَّـٰغُوتَ أَن يَعْبُدُوهَا وَأَنَابُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ لَهُمُ ٱلْبُشْرَىٰ ۚ فَبَشِّرْ عِبَادِ (۱۷)
As for those who stay clear of the worship of fake deities and turn penitently to Allah, there is good news for them. So give good news to My servants (17)
ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْتَمِعُونَ ٱلْقَوْلَ فَيَتَّبِعُونَ أَحْسَنَهُۥٓ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ هَدَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمْ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (۱۸)
who listen to the word [of Allah] and follow the best [interpretation] of it. They are the ones whom Allah has guided, and it is they who possess intellect. (18)

Key Points

"Tāghūt" is said of someone who is prone to transgression (ṭughyān) and aggression (tajāwuz). "Ijtināb" (avoidance) means placing something on one side and oneself on the other side. The meaning of turning (inābah) towards Allah and shunning Tāghūt is the same as what we read in Ayah al-Kursī: «فمَن یكفر بالطاغوت و یؤمن باللّه»(15) The meaning of worshipping Tāghūt is obeying oppressors and tyrants. This is because in a tradition found in Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān, we read: «مَن اطاع جبّاراً فقد عَبَده» (Whoever obeys a tyrant has worshipped him). Obedience to an oppressor is his worship. In Ayah 18, He says: Give glad tidings to those who follow the best (al-aḥsan), and elsewhere He considers the best speech (aḥsan) to be the call towards Allah, «احسن قولاً ممّن دعا الى اللّه»(16) And He considers the best caller to be the Messenger of Allah. «ادعوا الى اللّه على بصیرة»(17) Therefore, one must follow the best of sayings, which is the call towards Allah, and the best call towards Allah is the call of His Prophet. Thus, following the best (al-aḥsan) means following the revelation and the calls of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله.

15) Baqarah, 256. 16) Fussilat, 33. 17) Yusuf, 108.

Messages

1- Among the characteristics of the believers and the sincere servants of Allah is avoiding the Tāghūts. «اجتنبوا الطاغوت... فبشّر عباد»

2- Avoiding Tāghūt is the prerequisite for turning towards Allah. «اجتنبوا الطاغوت - انابوا الى اللّه»

3- The condition for repentance (tawbah) is distance from Tāghūt, and obedience to Tāghūt prevents turning (inābah) and repentance. «اجتنبوا الطاغوت - انابوا الى اللّه»

4- The blessings of choosing the best (al-aḥsan) are unlimited. (The word «بشّر» [give glad tidings] is absolute, so it includes all types of glad tidings).

5- Those who have the right to hear every speech are those who pay attention to the discourse itself and do not become afflicted by the personality of the speaker. «یستمعون القول»

6- Man must have the tolerance to hear the words of others and possess broad-mindedness (sa‘at-e sadr). «یستمعون القول...»

7- Islam has no anxiety about the presentation of others' speeches. «یستمعون القول»

8- Reason ('aql) is the inner proof (ḥujjat-e bāṭinī), and blind imitation is forbidden. «یستمعون القول فیتّبعون احسنه»

9- Choosing the best (al-aḥsan) is emphasized by the Quran. «یتبعون احسنه»

10- Those who have the right to hear every speech are those who possess the scientific and logical ability to distinguish the best (al-aḥsan). «یستمعون... فیتّبعون احسنه»

11- Speech that lacks goodness (ḥusn) is not worthy of listening (istimā‘). «یستمعون القول فیتبعون احسنه»

12- Let us not be satisfied with being good; let us pursue being better. «یتّبعون احسنه»

13- Choosing the best (al-aḥsan) is not possible without Divine success (tawfīq). «اولئك الّذین هداهم اللّه»

14- Divine guidance is received only by those who dedicate time to attain the truth and listen to the speeches. «اولئك الّذین هداهم اللّه»

15- Those who blindly accept a school of thought are, in reality, not people of intellect. «اولوا الالباب»

16- The only reliable path is conscious choice. «فیتبعون احسنه... اولئك الّذین هداهم اللّه»

Ayahs

أَفَمَنْ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِ كَلِمَةُ ٱلْعَذَابِ أَفَأَنتَ تُنقِذُ مَن فِى ٱلنَّارِ (۱۹)
Can he against whom the word of punishment has become due…? Can you rescue someone who is in the Fire? (19)
لَـٰكِنِ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ رَبَّهُمْ لَهُمْ غُرَفٌ مِّن فَوْقِهَا غُرَفٌ مَّبْنِيَّةٌ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۖ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ ۖ لَا يُخْلِفُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمِيعَادَ (۲٠)
But as for those who are wary of their Lord, for them there will be lofty abodes with [other] lofty abodes built above them, with streams running beneath them—a promise of Allah. Allah does not break His promise. (20)

Key Points

Ghuraf (غُرَف) is the plural of Ghurfah (غرفة), meaning water that rises from its place, or a structure built on an upper floor. The Prophet of Islam had great compassion for guiding stubborn individuals. Therefore, Allah says: Do not put yourself to such hardship, because Allah's tradition concerning this group is certain punishment, and their salvation is not in your hands.

Messages

1- Some people, through their obstinacy and stubbornness, close the windows of Divine forgiveness. «حقّ علیه كلمة العذاب»

2- Deviation is, in reality, fire. «تنقذ مَن فى النار»

3- Fear and hope must be side by side. In Ayah 16, we read: «لهم من فوقهم ظللٌ من النار» (For them are layers of fire above them), meaning layers of fire are above the heads of the inhabitants of Hellfire. And in this Ayah, we read: «لهم غرف من فوقها غرف» (For them are chambers above which are chambers), meaning for the people of piety there are chambers above which are other chambers.

4- There is no deviation in Allah's glad tidings and promises, but regarding Allah's warnings and threats, perhaps forgiveness and grace may intervene, and they may not be carried out. «وعداللّه لایخلف اللّه المیعاد»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ أَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَسَلَكَهُۥ يَنَـٰبِيعَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ يُخْرِجُ بِهِۦ زَرْعًا مُّخْتَلِفًا أَلْوَٰنُهُۥ ثُمَّ يَهِيجُ فَتَرَىٰهُ مُصْفَرًّا ثُمَّ يَجْعَلُهُۥ حُطَـٰمًا ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَذِكْرَىٰ لِأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (۲۱)
Have you not seen that Allah sends down water from the sky, then He conducts it through the ground as springs. Then He brings forth with it crops of diverse hues. Then they wither and you see them turn yellow. Then He turns them into chaff. There is indeed a lesson in that for those who possess intellect. (21)

Key Points

| Salk (سلك) is derived from sulūk (سلوك), meaning entry and penetration, and yanābī‘ (ینابیع) is the plural of yanbū‘ (ینبوع), meaning spring (fountain). Yahīj (یهیج) is derived from hayajān (هیجان), meaning rapid, fervent, and tumultuous movement. When this word is attributed to crops and vegetation, it means the beginning of drying out. Huṭām (حُطام) means fragments of dried plants and straw. Albāb (ألباب) is the plural of lubb (لُبّ), meaning core, essence, and intellect (reason).

Messages

1- We should not pass by the phenomena of nature heedlessly. «اَلَم تر» 2- One of the paths to knowing Allah is careful observation of phenomena. «اَلَم تر» 3- Rain from the sky is the source of springs and underground waters. «انزل من السماء ماء فسلكه ینابیع» 4- Natural factors are the medium and channel for the will of Allah. (Growing plants is the work of God, but through water). «یخرج به زرعا» 5- The diverse colors of plants, fruits, and flowers that grow from a single source of water and soil are signs of Allah's power. «زرعاً مختلفاً الوانه» 6- The creation of all phenomena is by Divine will. «سلكه - یخرج - یجعله» 7- Those who do not reflect upon the source of existence and its purpose are senseless. «لذكرى لاولى الالباب» 8- Accepting admonition is a sign of intellect. «ذكرى لاولى الالباب» 9- The believer is wise, and the disbeliever is obstinate and senseless. «لذكرى لاولى الالباب»

Ayah

أَفَمَن شَرَحَ ٱللَّهُ صَدْرَهُۥ لِلْإِسْلَـٰمِ فَهُوَ عَلَىٰ نُورٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۚ فَوَيْلٌ لِّلْقَـٰسِيَةِ قُلُوبُهُم مِّن ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۲۲)
Is someone whose breast Allah has opened to Islam so that he follows a light from His Lord…? So woe to those whose hearts have been hardened to the remembrance of Allah. They are in manifest error. (22)

Key Points

Regarding the cure for hardness of heart (qaswat al-qalb), it is mentioned in narrations that when the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله recited this Ayah, he was asked: What is the sign of light entering the heart? He replied: «التّجافى عن دار الغرور و الانابة الى دار الخلود و الاستعداد للموت»(18) (meaning) Detaching the heart from the deceptive abode of the world, turning toward the eternal abode of the Hereafter, and preparing for death before its arrival.

18) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The requirement of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah) is the guidance of individuals who accept the truth and possess breadth of heart. «هو على نور من ربّه»

2- Belief in religion and submission to the truth require a lofty spirit, which is a Divine gift. «شرح اللّه صدره للاسلام»

3- The one who possesses breadth of heart (sharh al-sadr) distinguishes truth from falsehood by means of Divine light. «هو على نور من ربّه»

4- Hardness of heart (qaswat al-qalb) prevents the shining of Divine light upon the heart. «القاسیة قلوبهم» (Instead of using "narrowness of heart" [tangdeli], which is more appropriate when contrasted with breadth of heart [sa'at al-sadr], the term "hardness of heart" [sangdeli] is used, because a narrow vessel is somewhat permeable, but a hard stone heart is never permeable.)

5- The people of submission (Ahl al-Taslim) all share one goal and one path, whereas the deviated ones each have their own goal. (The word sadr [chest/heart] is used in the singular, while qulubuhum [their hearts] is used in the plural.)

6- The light of the heart and breadth of heart (sa'at al-sadr) are from Allah. «شرح اللّه صدره للاسلام فهو على نور من ربّه» But man himself brings about deviation. «للقاسیة قلوبهم من ذكر اللّه»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ نَزَّلَ أَحْسَنَ ٱلْحَدِيثِ كِتَـٰبًا مُّتَشَـٰبِهًا مَّثَانِىَ تَقْشَعِرُّ مِنْهُ جُلُودُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ ثُمَّ تَلِينُ جُلُودُهُمْ وَقُلُوبُهُمْ إِلَىٰ ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُدَى ٱللَّهِ يَهْدِى بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِنْ هَادٍ (۲۳)
Allah has sent down the best of discourses, a scripture [composed] of similar motifs, whereat shiver the skins of those who fear their Lord, then their skins and hearts relax at Allah’s remembrance. That is Allah’s guidance, by which He guides whomever He wishes; and whomever Allah leads astray, has no guide. (23)

Key Points

"Hadith" means speech and discourse. The meaning of hadith in this Ayah refers to the Quran. The Quran being the "Ahsan al-Hadith" (Best Discourse) is due to its comprehensiveness, truthfulness, firmness, eloquence, and rhetoric. The word mutashābih (متشابه) itself is mutashābih, meaning it has several meanings. One meaning is the ambiguity of some verses of the Quran, in contrast to the clear and decisive verses (muḥkamāt). In this sense, mutashābih is a characteristic of some verses: «هنّ امّ الكتاب و اخر متشابهات» (19) However, mutashābih in this Ayah means the resemblance of the verses to one another, and thus it is a characteristic of all the verses. Mathānī (مثانى) is the plural of mathnā, meaning inclination or tendency. This means the verses of the Quran incline toward one another, and some verses interpret others. In other words, they repeat a single concept using different wordings.

19) Āl ‘Imrān, 7.

Messages

1- The Quran is the best of discourses. «احسن الحدیث» (Because it is the most truthful of speeches, «و من أصدق من اللّه حدیثا» (20), and it is the final word and the decisive statement. «فبأىّ حدیث بعده یؤمنون» (21))

2- All verses of the Quran resemble one another, and there is no inconsistency or contradiction among them. «متشابهاً»

3- The fear of God is manifest in the physical appearance and bodies of the believers; their outward state reflects their inner character. «تقشعرّ منه جلود الّذین یخشون ربّهم»

4- The believer feels fear upon hearing the verses of punishment, «تقشعرّ», and becomes hopeful upon hearing the verses of mercy. «ثمّ تلین جلودهم»

5- The Quran is the Book of Divine guidance. «كتاباً... ذلك هدى اللّه»

6- Allah has provided the means of guidance for everyone, but only a group accepts this guidance. «ذلك هدى اللّه... یهدى... یضلل»

20) An-Nisā’, 87.

21) Al-A‘rāf, 185.

Ayahs

أَفَمَن يَتَّقِى بِوَجْهِهِۦ سُوٓءَ ٱلْعَذَابِ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ وَقِيلَ لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ ذُوقُوا۟ مَا كُنتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ (۲٤)
What! Is someone who fends off with his face the terrible punishment [meted out to him] on the Day of Resurrection…? And the wrongdoers will be told, ‘Taste what you used to earn.’ (24)
كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ فَأَتَىٰهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ (۲٥)
Those who were before them impugned [the apostles], whereat the punishment overtook them whence they were not aware. (25)
فَأَذَاقَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْخِزْىَ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَكْبَرُ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا۟ يَعْلَمُونَ (۲٦)
So Allah made them taste disgrace in the life of the world, and the punishment of the Hereafter will surely be greater, if they knew. (26)

Key Points

The word "wajh" (وجه) in "bi-wajhihi" (بوجهه) means face, but perhaps in this Ayah, it means method or path, similar to the Ayah "و لكلٍ ّ وِجهةٌ هو مولّیها" (22), where it does not mean face. That is, everyone, with the direction (wijhah), means, and power they possess, separates their path from evils and wards off danger from themselves.

(22) Al-Baqarah, 148.

Messages

1- The result of piety towards Allah in this world is remaining far from His wrath on the Day of Judgment. «یتّقى بوجهه»

2- One of the torments of the criminals is hearing humiliating words. «قیل للظالمین»

3- What afflicts man on the Day of Judgment are the sins he committed knowingly. «تكسِبون»

4- The history full of lessons concerning past disbelievers (kuffar) and tyrants (taghuts) is a warning for future generations. «مِن قبلهم»

5- The Hand of Allah is open in punishing criminals, and He can chastise them through unforeseen ways. «من حیث لا یشعرون»

6- Not all punishments are postponed until the Day of Judgment. «الخزى فى الحیاة الدنیا»

7- The punishment of the Hereafter is greater and more enduring. «لعذاب الاخرة اكبر»

8- The disbelievers (kuffar) and the deniers do not know the severity of the Day of Judgment, which is why they act in this manner. «لَوْ كانوا یعلمون»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ ضَرَبْنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِى هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانِ مِن كُلِّ مَثَلٍ لَّعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (۲۷)
We have drawn for mankind in this Quran every [kind of] example, so that they may take admonition: (27)
قُرْءَانًا عَرَبِيًّا غَيْرَ ذِى عِوَجٍ لَّعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ (۲۸)
an Arabic Quran, without any deviousness, so that they may be Godwary. (28)

Messages

1- What is important is guiding and reminding people, whether through argumentation or through parables. «ضربنا للناس... من كلّ مثل»

2- The effect of the parable on the general public is greater than that of argumentation. «للناس»

3- Every type of example related to reminding man and removing his heedlessness has been included in the Quran. «كل مثل - یتذكّرون»

4- Man possesses a pure nature and inner beliefs, but he becomes heedless of them, and removing heedlessness is necessary. «یتذكّرون»

5- The repetition of the word Quran in two consecutive verses is a sign that the Quran is a book that is readable, instructive, and salvific. «هذا القرآن... قرآناً عربیّاً»

6- Considering verse 23, which named the Quran Ahsan al-Hadith (the Best Discourse), it becomes clear that one of the signs of the best discourse is having illuminating examples and being free from deviation. «كل مثل - قرآنا عربیّاً - غیر ذى عوج»

7- Every writing is subject to change and requires evolution, and sooner or later its flaws appear (which is why all high-ranking authors apologize for weaknesses in the introduction of their books), except for the Quran, which is perfect and flawless. «غیر ذى عوج»

8- Piety (Taqwa) depends on constant attention and remembrance. «لعلهم یتذكّرون... لعلهم یتقون»

9- In cultural and religious endeavors, the probability of effect is sufficient, and it is not necessary to have 100% certainty regarding the impact of the work. «لعلهم یتذكّرون»

Ayah

ضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا رَّجُلًا فِيهِ شُرَكَآءُ مُتَشَـٰكِسُونَ وَرَجُلًا سَلَمًا لِّرَجُلٍ هَلْ يَسْتَوِيَانِ مَثَلًا ۚ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۲۹)
Allah draws an example: a man jointly owned by several contending masters, and a man belonging entirely to one man: are the two equal in comparison? All praise belongs to Allah! But most of them do not know. (29)

Key Points

In the previous verses, He said: We have presented every kind of example in the Quran so that people may be reminded. This verse presents one of the best examples that illustrates the portrait of the bewildered polytheists. The example in this verse is similar to the example that Prophet Yusuf (Joseph) presented to the polytheists in prison when he said: «ءارباب متفرّقون خیرٌ أم اللّه الواحد القهّار»(23) Is accepting multiple masters better, or submitting to the One, the All-Dominant God? «متشاكِسون» (mutashākisūn) means ill-tempered partners who are constantly quarreling with one another.

23) Yusuf, 39.

Messages

1- The monotheist is only concerned with the satisfaction of the One God, but the polytheist is constantly thinking about satisfying several people. «فیه شركاء - سَلَماً لرجل»

2- In religious discussions, there is no impediment to giving examples concerning God, just as in this verse, submission to God is likened to the submission of a slave to his master. «سلماً لرجل»

3- Apart from God and the path of God, all individuals and paths lead to contradiction and conflict, because desires and tastes are vastly different. «شركاء متشاكِسون»

4- One of the occasions requiring gratitude (shukr) is the clarification of truths. «هل یستَوِیان - الحمدللّه»

5- The difference between the tranquility of the believer and the confusion of the polytheist is clear to reason and innate nature (fitrah). «هل یستوِیان»

6- Most people are unaware of the harms of polytheism (shirk). «بل اكثرهم لا یعلمون»

Ayahs

إِنَّكَ مَيِّتٌ وَإِنَّهُم مَّيِّتُونَ (۳٠)
You will die, and they [too] will die. (30)
ثُمَّ إِنَّكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ عِندَ رَبِّكُمْ تَخْتَصِمُونَ (۳۱)
Then on the Day of Resurrection you will contend before your Lord. (31)

Key Points

A sample of the people's contention on the Day of Resurrection: The misguided oppressed (mustad'afūn) will say to the arrogant ones (mustakbirūn): «لولا انتم لكنّا مؤمنین»(24) If it were not for you, we would have been believers. The leaders of disbelief will say to the misguided ones: «قالوا بل لم تكونوا مؤمنین»(25) Rather, you yourselves were not people of faith. And they will also say: «أنحن صددناكم عن الهُدى»(26) Did we prevent you from guidance?

24) Saba', 31. 25) Saffat, 29. 26) Saba', 32.

Messages

1- The popularity of individuals does not prevent the execution of Divine traditions (Sunan), one of which is death. «انّك میّت»

2- Prophets are like others in ordinary life. «انك میّت»

3- Speak frankly even with loved ones. «انك میّت»

4- When conveying harsh and bitter messages, start first with those close to you. «انك میّت و انّهم میّتون»

5- There are different stages on the Day of Resurrection:

In one stage, people dispute with one another. «تختصمون»

In another stage, the address comes: «لا تختصموا لدىّ»(27) Do not dispute in My presence.

And in yet another stage, their lips will be sealed. «نختم على افواههم»(28) 27) Qaf, 28. 28) Ya Sin, 65.

Ayah

۞ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن كَذَبَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ وَكَذَّبَ بِٱلصِّدْقِ إِذْ جَآءَهُۥٓ ۚ أَلَيْسَ فِى جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوًى لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۳۲)
So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who attributes a falsehood to Allah, and denies the truth when it reaches him? Is not the [final] abode of the faithless in hell? (32)

Key Points

The meaning of al-ṣidq (the truth) in the noble Ayah is the word of Allah, which was revealed to the Prophet and presented to the people in the form of the Quran. The word mathwā (مثوى) derives from thawā (ثواء), meaning a place of permanent residence.

Messages

1- Oppression against the thought and culture of society is the worst form of oppression. «فمن اظلم ممّن كذَب على اللّه»

2- The disbelievers denied the truth the very moment they heard it, without any hesitation or reflection. «كَذَب... اِذ جاءَ»

3- In the method of propagation, awaken consciences through questioning. «فمن اظلم - ألیس فى جهنّم»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِى جَآءَ بِٱلصِّدْقِ وَصَدَّقَ بِهِۦٓ ۙ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ (۳۳)
He who brings the truth and he who confirms it—it is they who are the Godwary. (33)
لَهُم مَّا يَشَآءُونَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۳٤)
They will have what they wish with their Lord—that is the reward of the virtuous— (34)

Key Points

Truthfulness (صدق) has dimensions: Truthfulness in speech: «جاء بالصدق» (the Ayah under discussion). Truthfulness in promise: «انّه كان صادق الوعد»(29). Truthfulness in covenant: «صدقوا ما عاهدوا اللّه علیه»(30). Truthfulness in action: «اولئك الّذین صدقوا»(31). And whoever possesses the necessary sincerity in all stages is called a Siddiq (the truthful one).

29) Maryam, 54. 30) Al-Ahzab, 23. 31) Al-Baqarah, 177.

Messages

1- The preacher of religion is praiseworthy only when he himself acts upon what he preaches. «جاء بالصّدق و صدّق به»

2- Propagating religion is not exclusive to the Prophet. «جاء بالصّدق... اولئك» (The word «اولئك» [those] is used, not «هو» [he]). As He says elsewhere: «أدعوا الى اللّه على بصيرة أنا و مَن اتّبعنى»(32) I and everyone who follows me call towards Allah with insight.

3- One of the signs of the righteous (Muttaqin) is the propagation of the Divine message. «جاء بالصدق... متّقون»

4- The means of receiving blessings in Paradise is the will and desire of the inhabitants of Paradise. «لهم ما یشاؤن»

5- Divine favors in Paradise have no limits. «لهم ما یشاؤن»

6- The righteous (Muttaqin) are the same as the doers of good (Muhsinin). «هم المتّقون - المحسنین» 32) Yusuf, 108.

Ayah

لِيُكَفِّرَ ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ أَسْوَأَ ٱلَّذِى عَمِلُوا۟ وَيَجْزِيَهُمْ أَجْرَهُم بِأَحْسَنِ ٱلَّذِى كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۳٥)
that Allah may absolve them of the worst of what they did, and pay them their reward by the best of what they used to do. (35)

Messages

1- One of the rewards for the propagators of religion is divine pardon and the overlooking of their slips. «جاء بالصّدق و صدّق به... لیكفّر اللّه عنهم»

2- The status of piety (Taqwa) and goodness (Ihsan) does not contradict the commission of some ugly deeds. (The pronoun «عنهم» refers back to the same pious ones (muttaqin) and good-doers (muhsinin) in the previous Ayah). «لیكفّر اللّه عنهم أسوء الّذى عملوا»

3- It is sufficient of Allah's grace that He covers the worst sins and rewards in the best manner. «یكفر اللّه عنهم اسوأ - یجزیهم اجرهم باحسن»

4- Purification from sin and its effects is a prerequisite for receiving divine favors. (First «یُكفّر» [He covers], then «یجزیهم» [He rewards them]).

5- Allah rewards the pious (muttaqin) and the good-doers (muhsinin) based on the value of their best deed. (He rewards their average deeds as if they were their best deeds). «بأحسن الّذى...»

Ayahs

أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِكَافٍ عَبْدَهُۥ ۖ وَيُخَوِّفُونَكَ بِٱلَّذِينَ مِن دُونِهِۦ ۚ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِنْ هَادٍ (۳٦)
Does not Allah suffice [to defend] His servant? They would frighten you of others than Him. Yet whomever Allah leads astray, has no guide, (36)
وَمَن يَهْدِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِن مُّضِلٍّ ۗ أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِعَزِيزٍ ذِى ٱنتِقَامٍ (۳۷)
and whomever Allah guides, there is no one who can lead him astray. Is not Allah an all-mighty avenger? (37)

Key Points

The idolaters used to tell the Prophet of Islam: If you insult our idols, harm and affliction will befall you. This Ayah was revealed, comforting the Prophet that: «ألیس اللّه بكافٍ عبده» (Is not Allah sufficient for His servant?) The Quran has addressed the immunity of the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله from various conspiracies through different expressions, such as the Ayah «و اللّه یعصمك من الناس»(33) and «انّا كفیناك المستهزئین»(34). Just as Allah protected Noah from drowning, Abraham from the fire, Moses from the danger of Pharaoh, and Jesus from being crucified. Our duty in all circumstances is servitude to Allah. We read in a Hadith: «مَن أصبح و همومه هَمٌّ واحد، كفاه اللّه هموم الدنیا والآخرة»(35) (He whose sole concern and effort is the servitude of Allah, Allah will fulfill his needs in this world and the Hereafter.)

33) Al-Ma'idah, 67. 34) Al-Hijr, 95. 35) Tafsir Ruh al-Bayan.

Messages

1- If a person becomes a servant of Allah, he is insured, and Allah suffices and guarantees his affairs. «ألیس اللّه بكاف عبده»

2- In the face of enemies' threats, we should calm ourselves by remembering divine aid and guarantee. «ألیس اللّه بكافٍ عبده و یخوّفونك»

3- The tool of the disbelievers is the creation of an atmosphere of dread and terror. «یخوفونك بالّذین من دونه»

4- Guidance and misguidance are in the hand of Allah, «یضلل اللّه - یهد اللّه», but the prerequisites for receiving guidance or being deprived of it are in the hands of man. As we read in other verses: «یهدى اللّه من ینیب»(36) and «لا یهدى من هو مسرف كذّاب»(37)

5- Do not fear the scarcity of numbers and resources; fear departing from the path of servitude to Allah, for if you are His servant, no power can break you. «فما له من مضل»

6- Man is a being who, in hardness of heart, reaches a point where no guide is effective for him. «فما له مِن هاد» And in faith, he reaches a point where no event can lead him astray. «فماله من مضل»

7- Man's misguidance by Allah is not without prerequisite; it is the recompense for man's own actions. «ذى انتقام»

8- Allah's support is sometimes in the form of guaranteeing the servant and sometimes in the form of vengeance against the opponents. «ذى انتقام»

36) Ash-Shura, 13. 37) Ghafir, 28.

Ayah

وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُم مَّنْ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ لَيَقُولُنَّ ٱللَّهُ ۚ قُلْ أَفَرَءَيْتُم مَّا تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ إِنْ أَرَادَنِىَ ٱللَّهُ بِضُرٍّ هَلْ هُنَّ كَـٰشِفَـٰتُ ضُرِّهِۦٓ أَوْ أَرَادَنِى بِرَحْمَةٍ هَلْ هُنَّ مُمْسِكَـٰتُ رَحْمَتِهِۦ ۚ قُلْ حَسْبِىَ ٱللَّهُ ۖ عَلَيْهِ يَتَوَكَّلُ ٱلْمُتَوَكِّلُونَ (۳۸)
If you ask them, ‘Who created the heavens and the earth?’ they will surely say, ‘Allah.’ Say, ‘Have you considered what you invoke besides Allah? Should Allah desire some distress for me, can they remove the distress visited by Him? Or should He desire some mercy for me, can they withhold His mercy?’ Say, ‘Allah is sufficient for me. In Him let all the trusting put their trust.’ (38)

Key Points

The Qur'an repeatedly mentions the polytheists' belief in Allah as the Creator. In addition to the current Ayah under discussion, refer to: Surah Al-'Ankabut, Ayahs 61 to 63; Surah Luqman, Ayah 24; and Surah Az-Zukhruf, Ayahs 9 and 87.

The word "ضُرٌّ" (ḍurr), in contrast to mercy (raḥmah), means any kind of affliction or distress.

The pronoun "هُنّ" (hunna) is feminine plural and is used here for the idols, because the Arabs referred to major idols by feminine names, such as: "لات" (Lat), "مناة" (Manat), and "عُزّى" (Uzza).

Messages

1- Idolaters accepted Allah's role as the Creator. (However, they attributed the roles of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and intercession to the idols). «لیقولنّ اللَّه»

2- Idols have no role whatsoever against the Will of Allah. «هل هنّ كاشفات - هل هنّ مُمسِكات»

3- Only He who has the power to bring benefit and repel harm is worthy of worship. «هل هنّ كاشفات - هل هنّ مُمسِكات»

4- Repelling harm is more important than attracting benefit. («كاشفات ضُرّ» [Removers of distress] precedes «مُمسِكات رحمته» [Holders back of His mercy]).

5- Reliance (Tawakkul) is permissible only upon Allah. (The word «علیه» [upon Him] precedes «یتوكّل» [let them rely], indicating exclusivity).

6- Allah suffices and takes care of His Prophet in every respect. «حَسْبى اللّه» (The word «حَسْبى» [sufficient for me] is used absolutely, encompassing everything).

Ayahs

قُلْ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنِّى عَـٰمِلٌ ۖ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ (۳۹)
Say, ‘O my people! Act according to your ability. I too am acting. Soon you will know (39)
مَن يَأْتِيهِ عَذَابٌ يُخْزِيهِ وَيَحِلُّ عَلَيْهِ عَذَابٌ مُّقِيمٌ (٤٠)
who will be overtaken by a punishment that will disgrace him, and on whom a lasting punishment will descend.’ (40)

Key Points

The phrase "فسوف تعلمون" (then you will know) implies that the command "اعملوا" (work/act) is a sign of threat and for reproach, not permission or authorization. "مكانت" (makānah) means status and rank, which is used for non-sensory matters, just as the word "مكان" (makān) is used for sensory matters. Of course, it might also be derived from muknah, meaning ability and power. The intent of "عذابٌ یخزیه" (a humiliating punishment) is the punishment of this world, and the intent of "عذابٌ مقیم" (an enduring punishment) is the punishment of the Hereafter.(38)

38) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The leader must have a decisive approach while simultaneously showing affection. ("یا قومِ" (O my people) is a sign of affection, and "سوف تعلمون" (you will soon know) is a sign of decisiveness).

2- Faith and reliance (tawakkul) upon Allah are the support for taking a stand against enemies. "حسبى اللّه علیه یتوكّل... سوف تعلمون" (Allah is sufficient for me; upon Him rely... you will soon know).

3- Everyone is accountable for their own deeds. "اعملوا - انى عامل" (Work/Act - I am working/acting).

4- Divine leaders did not retreat an iota and made the disbelievers despair of compromise. "انّى عامل" (I am working/acting).

Ayah

إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ لِلنَّاسِ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ فَمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ فَلِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن ضَلَّ فَإِنَّمَا يَضِلُّ عَلَيْهَا ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْهِم بِوَكِيلٍ (٤۱)
Indeed We have sent down the Book to you with the truth for [the deliverance of] mankind. So whoever is guided is guided for his own sake, and whoever goes astray, goes astray to his own detriment, and it is not your duty to watch over them. (41)

Key Points

Contrary to those who assume that certain contents were imparted to the Prophet of Islam and that he then conveyed those contents to the people using his own expressions, the Quran considers the very words of the Book to be revealed from the Unseen (Ghayb). «انّا انزلنا علیك الكتاب»

Messages

  1. The Quran existed in the form of a book during the time of the Prophet. «انزلنا علیك الكتاب»

  2. The revelation of the Book is for all people. «للناس»

  3. Both the contents of the Quran are entirely truth (Haqq), and no distortion or falsehood has found its way into it, and the revelation of such a Book is necessary, worthy, and true (Haqq). «بالحقّ»

  4. Man is free in choosing his path. «مَن اهتدى - مَن ضلّ»

  5. Allah and the Messenger have no need for our faith. «فمن اهتدى فلنفسه...»

  6. The duty of the Prophet is conveyance (of the message), not compulsion. (Even the Prophets do not have the right to impose belief). «و ما انت علیهم بوكیل»

  7. True and complete guidance is only found under the shadow of the Divine Book. «انا انزلنا علیك الكتاب... فمن اهتدى »

  8. Allah consoles and reassures His Prophet in the face of the stubbornness of the disbelievers. «و ما انت علیهم بوكیل»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ يَتَوَفَّى ٱلْأَنفُسَ حِينَ مَوْتِهَا وَٱلَّتِى لَمْ تَمُتْ فِى مَنَامِهَا ۖ فَيُمْسِكُ ٱلَّتِى قَضَىٰ عَلَيْهَا ٱلْمَوْتَ وَيُرْسِلُ ٱلْأُخْرَىٰٓ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (٤۲)
Allah takes the souls at the time of their death, and those who have not died, in their sleep. Then He retains those for whom He has ordained death and releases the others until a specified time. There are indeed signs in that for a people who reflect. (42)

Key Points

Yatawaffā (یَتوفّى) is derived from wafā (وَفى), meaning the complete reception of a matter, and in the context of death, it means taking the soul completely.

Question: In Ayah 61 of Surah Al-An'am, it says: «توفّته رسلنا» (Our messengers take the soul), and in Ayah 11 of Surah As-Sajdah, it says: «یتوفّاكم ملك الموت» (The Angel of Death takes your soul), and this Ayah says: God takes the souls. Which is correct?

Answer: Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah has appointed helpers from among the angels for Azrael, "Malak al-Mawt" (the Angel of Death), who take the souls of the people and hand them over to him, and he delivers the souls, along with what He personally seized, to Allah.(39)

We read in a hadith: During sleep, the Rūḥ (spirit) remains in the body, but the Nafs (soul/self) ascends to the heavens. The relationship between the Nafs and the Rūḥ is like the relationship between the sun and the sun's ray. If Allah wills and the time of death has arrived, the Rūḥ also separates from the body and joins the Nafs; otherwise, the Nafs descends from the heavens and rejoins the Rūḥ.(40)

Question: When the Rūḥ separates from the body at the time of death, where does it go?

Answer: At the time of death, the Rūḥ joins a body similar to this one, which is called the Qālib Mithālī (the subtle/exemplary form), and in Barzakh (the intermediate realm), it experiences bliss or torment with that body. Even in sleep, the Rūḥ sees dreams and travels around with that light body, the Qālib Mithālī. The human body is like a car, and the Rūḥ is like its driver. Sometimes the car is running and the driver is behind the wheel; this is the state of wakefulness. Sometimes the car is running, but the driver gets out and leaves; this is the state of sleep. This is because the heart, stomach, and kidneys are working, but the Rūḥ separates and joins a similar body, traveling around. This is the same body with which we travel in our dreams, named the Qālib Mithālī, and it is so light and agile that it can fly in the heavens, swim in the oceans, and travel around the world in an instant without any intermediary. Sometimes both the car is off and the driver has separated; this is the state of death.(41)

In the sayings of the religious leaders, death and the Day of Judgment have also been likened to sleep and wakefulness. We read in a hadith: «كما تنومون تموتون» (Just as you sleep, you die), and just as you wake up, you will be brought back to life. Luqman the Wise used to tell his son: If you had the power to ward off sleep and wakefulness from yourself, you would also be able to ward off death and the Resurrection!(42)

(39) Bihar, Vol. 6, p. 144. (40) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. (41) I have included material on this topic in the discussion of Barzakh in my book, Ma'ad (The Resurrection). (42) Tafsir Makhzan al-Irfan.

Messages

1- The body and the Rūḥ (spirit) are two independent realities, and they separate from each other during death or sleep, and the Rūḥ remains after death. «اللّه یتوفّى الانفس»

2- Death and sleep are siblings of one another. «حین موتها - فى منامها»

3- If we know that we surrender our souls every night, we distance ourselves from arrogance, heedlessness, and sin. «و الّتى لم تمت فى منامها»

4- Our death and life are in the hands of Allah. «اللّه یتوفّى - قضى علیها الموت - یرسل...»

5- No one remains alive forever. «یرسل الاخرى الى اجلٍ مسمّى»

6- Sleep and wakefulness are for all human beings, but only people of thought learn lessons from them. «لآیات لقوم یتفكّرون»

Ayah

أَمِ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ شُفَعَآءَ ۚ قُلْ أَوَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يَعْقِلُونَ (٤۳)
Have they taken intercessors besides Allah? Say, ‘What! Even though they do not control anything, and cannot reason?!’ (43)

Key Points

An intercessor (Shafi') must have permission from Allah for intercession, [as stated in the verse] «مَن ذا الّذى یشفع عنده الاّ باذنه» (Who is it that can intercede with Him except by His permission?). But what permission do idols have? An intercessor must be beloved by Allah and acceptable to Him, whereas idols are neither beloved nor acceptable to Him. In Ayah 18 of Surah Yunus, we read that the polytheists say: «هؤلاء شفعائنا» (These are our intercessors). And in Ayah 3 of the same Surah, we read that they say: «ما نعبدهم الاّ لیقرّبونا الى اللّه زلفى» (We only worship them so that they may bring us nearer to Allah in proximity). The worship of idols is because the idols bring us closer to Allah and act as our intercessors. This Ayah states: How can you expect help from beings that possess neither intellect nor power?! «كانوا لا یَملكون شیئا و لا یعقلون» (They possess nothing and do not reason/understand). When we seek tawassul (means of approach) through the infallible Imams علیهم السلام and seek aid and intercession from them, it is because we have understood their comprehension and awareness from the verses and narrations that prove the life of Barzakh (the intermediate realm) for the friends of Allah, and we have believed in the power of those great personalities by witnessing thousands of practical examples in the answering of prayers, miracles, and divine favors (karamat).

Messages

1- Some polytheists did not consider idols to be God, but rather considered them intercessors and mediators before Allah. «اتّخذوا من دون اللّه شفعاء»

2- A mediator between man and Allah must understand the need and possess the power to help, neither of which the idols possess. «لا یملكون - لا یعقلون»

Ayah

قُل لِّلَّهِ ٱلشَّفَـٰعَةُ جَمِيعًا ۖ لَّهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (٤٤)
Say, ‘All intercession rests with Allah. To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth; then you will be brought back to Him.’ (44)

Key Points

| Question: Is the love and affection of others greater than Allah's, such that Allah wills punishment, but the intercessors intercede like a wet nurse kinder than a mother? Answer: This Ayah states: all intercessions belong exclusively to Allah. And if a Prophet or an Imam intercedes, shows compassion, or kindness, the source of all those favors and affections is from His sacred Essence and by His permission, «ما من شفیع الاّ من بعد اذنه»(43), and the path of those favors is through the Divine Saints. «قل للّه الشفاعة جمیعا»

43) Yunus, 10.

Messages

1- The educational method of the Quran is to eliminate all corrupt motives and concentrate all motives on seeking Allah. Therefore, to those who go around seeking honor from others, He says: «انّ العزّه للّه جمیعاً»(44). All honor belongs to Allah; why do you revolve around others?! To those who go around seeking power from others, He says: «انّ القوّه للّه جمیعاً»(45). To those who go around seeking intermediaries from others, He says: «قل للّه الشفاعة جمیعاً».

2- Existence and all factors involved in it are in the hand of Allah. «للّه الشفاعة... له ملك السموات» 44) Yunus, 65. 45) Al-Baqarah, 165.

Ayah

وَإِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ ٱشْمَأَزَّتْ قُلُوبُ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ وَإِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ إِذَا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ (٤٥)
When Allah is mentioned alone, [thereat] shrink away the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter, but when others are mentioned besides Him, behold, they rejoice! (45)

Key Points

Although the addressees of this Ayah are those who do not believe in the Hereafter, there are also individuals among Muslims who feel anxiety and aversion towards pure Islam. The moment we say, "Qisas (retaliation) is a divine law," their faces frown, but the moment we say, "International Law," they smile. Whenever he stands for prayer, where his sole addressee is Allah, his soul is averse; but whenever he gives a speech or teaches, where his addressee is other than Allah, he speaks with full attention and enthusiasm. A similar Ayah is found in Ayah 46 of Surah Al-Isra: «و اذا ذكرت ربّك فى القرآن وحده ولّوا على ادبارهم نفورا» (And when you mention your Lord in the Qur'an alone, they turn their backs in aversion.) When you mention your Lord in the Qur'an alone, they turn their backs and turn away from you.

Messages

1- Let us test the extent of our belief and faith in the Hereafter through our aversion or attention towards the decrees of Allah. «اشمأزّت... یستبشرون»

2- There is a close relationship between Monotheism (Tawhid) and the Resurrection (Ma'ad). «ذكر اللّه... اشمأزت قلوب الّذین لا یؤمنون بالاخرة»

3- The remembrance of Allah, which is a source of tranquility for hearts, is a source of distress and annoyance for some groups. «اشمأزّت»

Ayah

قُلِ ٱللَّهُمَّ فَاطِرَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ عَـٰلِمَ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ أَنتَ تَحْكُمُ بَيْنَ عِبَادِكَ فِى مَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ (٤٦)
Say, ‘O Allah! Originator of the heavens and the earth, Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, You will judge between Your servants concerning that about which they used to differ.’ (46)

Messages

1- Preachers of religion and divine leaders who face stubborn disbelievers must always seek help from Allah through His remembrance. «قل الّلهم»

2- In the face of individuals who have devoted their hearts to other than Allah and detest mentioning the name of Allah, we should turn our attention to Him. «قل الّلهم»

3- The beginning and end of existence belong to Him, so why are some delighted by others besides Him? «من دونه اذا هم یستبشرون - فاطر السموات و الارض»

4- Allah's judgment on the Day of Resurrection is based on His knowledge of the manifest and the hidden. «عالم الغیب و الشهادة... تحكم بین عبادك»

5- The creation of the heavens is more important than the creation of the earth. (The name of the heavens is always mentioned before the name of the earth). «السموات و الارض»

6- Allah's knowledge of the unseen (Ghayb) and the witnessed (Shahadah) is the same. «عالم الغیب و الشهادة»

7- People, despite all the differences they have, are His servants. «عبادك فیما كانوا فیه یختلفون»

8- The dispute between polytheists and monotheists will always exist. «كانوا فیه یختلفون»

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا وَمِثْلَهُۥ مَعَهُۥ لَٱفْتَدَوْا۟ بِهِۦ مِن سُوٓءِ ٱلْعَذَابِ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ وَبَدَا لَهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يَكُونُوا۟ يَحْتَسِبُونَ (٤۷)
Even if the wrongdoers possessed all that is on the earth, and as much of it besides, they would offer it on the Day of Resurrection to redeem themselves with it from a terrible punishment, and there will appear to them from Allah what they had never reckoned. (47)

Key Points

In the Resurrection, rewards will appear for the early-rising believers that no one is aware of, "فلا تعلم نفس ما اخفى لهم من قرّة اعین" (46). And for the oppressors, matters will appear that they never thought of. "و بدا لهم من اللّه مالم یكونوا یحتسبون" And on that Day, it will be said to man: You were heedless, and today We have removed the veil, and you have become sharp-sighted (47).

46) Sajdah, 17. 47) Qaf, 22.

Messages

1- In the Resurrection, no one possesses wealth or fortune to save himself. "لو"

2- In the Divine Court, nothing prevents the conviction of the criminals. "تحكم بین عبادك - لو انّ للّذین ظلموا..."

3- The instinct of self-preservation prevails over the love of wealth. "ما فى الارض... لافتدوا به"

4- If today we commit a transgression for a single dirham, we should know that tomorrow, even twice the entire wealth of the earth will not be effective for our salvation. "و مثله معه"

5- Allah's judgment is accompanied by the revelation of man's own errors, so that there is no room for denial. "تحكم بین عبادك... و بدا لهم"

6- Human calculations are neither comprehensive nor realistic. "بدا لهم من اللّه مالم یكونوا یحتسبون"

Ayah

وَبَدَا لَهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتُ مَا كَسَبُوا۟ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (٤۸)
The evils of what they had earned will appear to them, and they will be besieged by what they used to deride. (48)

Messages

1- The Day of Resurrection is the day of uncovering and manifestation, and all secrets will become manifest. «بدا لهم»

2- The manifestation of the offense before the offender is his first spiritual torment. «بدا لهم سیّئات ما كسبوا»

3- By Divine power, first what was not in the calculations of the oppressors is revealed, then what they themselves considered bad. «بدا لهم مالم تكونوا... بدا لهم سیّئات»

4- A transgression becomes a crime when it is accompanied by oppression, mockery, intentionality, and continuity. «ظلموا - كسبوا - كانوا به یستهزؤن»

5- The Divine wrath encompasses the entire being of the oppressors. «حاق بهم»

Ayah

فَإِذَا مَسَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ ضُرٌّ دَعَانَا ثُمَّ إِذَا خَوَّلْنَـٰهُ نِعْمَةً مِّنَّا قَالَ إِنَّمَآ أُوتِيتُهُۥ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍۭ ۚ بَلْ هِىَ فِتْنَةٌ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٤۹)
When distress befalls man, he supplicates Us. Then, when We grant him a blessing from Us, he says, ‘I was given it by virtue of [my] knowledge.’ Indeed, it is a test, but most of them do not know. (49)

Key Points

In the Quran, this point is mentioned repeatedly: that when a human being faces danger, he turns to Allah, but as soon as he is saved from the danger, he forgets God. This is a sign of human ingratitude.

Messages

1- Man is a vulnerable being. «مسّ الانسان ضُرّ»

2- Man is so helpless that he cries out the moment danger reaches him. «مَسّ الانسان ضُرّ دَعانا»

3- Hardships are the result of human actions and a means of trial, but blessings are divine grace. «مسّ الانسان ضُرّ... خوّلناه نعمة منا»

4- Pressures and incidents make man acknowledge his own weakness and awaken and flourish the God-seeking nature (Fitrah) within him. «فاذا مسّ الانسان ضُرّ دعانا»

5- Sincere supplication attracts Allah's mercy. «دعانا... خوّلناه نعمة»

6- Prosperity and comfort are the grounds for heedlessness and arrogance. «خوّلناه نعمة... انّما اوتیته على علمٍ»

7- Man is an ungrateful being. «خوّلناه نعمة... انّما اوتیته على علمٍ»

8- What is criticized is ignoring Allah's grace, not that knowledge and science are ineffective in the growth and development of life. «قال انّما اوتیته»

9- Most people do not know that blessings and hardships are means of trial (test) so that the grateful and the ungrateful may be distinguished from one another. «بل هى فتنة ولكن اكثرهم لا یعلمون»

Ayahs

قَدْ قَالَهَا ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ فَمَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (٥٠)
Those who were before them [also] said that, but what they used to earn did not avail them. (50)
فَأَصَابَهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتُ مَا كَسَبُوا۟ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مِنْ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ سَيُصِيبُهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتُ مَا كَسَبُوا۟ وَمَا هُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ (٥۱)
So the evils of what they had earned visited them, and as for the wrongdoers among these, the evils of what they earn shall be visited on them and they will not frustrate [Allah]. (51)

Key Points

Ayah 50 generally states: what the criminals earned in this world will be of no use to them in the Hereafter. In other verses of the Quran, examples of what they earned are mentioned, including: «لن تغنى عنهم اموالهم»(48) their wealth will never be effective. «ما اغنى عنكم جمعكم»(49) their multitude will never be effective. «لا یغنى عنهم كیدهم»(50) their plotting will never be effective. Qarun also considered his capital the result of his knowledge, thought, and economic policy, saying: «انّما اوتیته على علم عندى»(51) Therefore, the Quran says in this Ayah: «قد قالها الّذین من قبلهم» this statement is not new.

48) Aal-i-Imran, 10. 49) Al-A'raf, 48. 50) At-Tur, 46. 51) Al-Qasas, 78.

Messages

1- We should utilize historical examples for guidance and education. «قد قالها الّذین من قبلهم»

2- History is the best witness that wealth and riches, at the time of Divine wrath, save man neither in this world nor in the Hereafter. «فما اغنى عنهم ما كانوا یكسبون» (When Qarun sank into the earth, no group could save him.)(52)

3- Divine traditions (Sunan) are governed by law, and time and place have no effect on them. Regarding the past: «اصابهم سیئات ما كسبوا» and regarding the future: «سیصیبهم سیّئات ما كسبوا»

4- The means of salvation in the Hereafter are matters other than wealth and power. «فما اغنى عنهم ما كانوا یكسبون»

5- Man's destiny is in his own hands. «فاصابهم سیّئات ما كسبوا»

6- Criminals are incapable of warding off Allah’s wrath from themselves. «سیصیبهم سیّئات ما كسبوا و ما هم بمعجزین» 52) Al-Qasas, 81.

Ayah

أَوَلَمْ يَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ (٥۲)
Do they not know that Allah expands the provision for whomever He wishes and tightens it [for whomever He wishes]? There are indeed signs in that for a people who have faith. (52)

Key Points

We read in Ayah 49 that man attributes divine blessings to his own knowledge and expertise. This Ayah states: This attribution is due to man's ignorance. In narrations (Riwāyāt), we read: Sometimes Allah restricts the provision (rizq) of intelligent people and increases the provision of less intelligent people, in order to prove that not everything is achieved through cleverness! (53)

53) Tafsir Ruh al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Although human effort, striving, and knowledge are necessary conditions for acquiring provision (rizq), they are not the sufficient condition or the ultimate cause. «انّ اللّه یبسط الرزق» (Let us not rely on our own resources, abilities, and information, for another power is at work).

Ayah

۞ قُلْ يَـٰعِبَادِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسْرَفُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ لَا تَقْنَطُوا۟ مِن رَّحْمَةِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَغْفِرُ ٱلذُّنُوبَ جَمِيعًا ۚ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (٥۳)
Say [that Allah declares,] ‘O My servants who have committed excesses against their own souls, do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed Allah will forgive all sins. Indeed, He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful. (53)

Key Points

The previous verses contained a threat for the arrogant individuals, and this verse gives glad tidings to the repentant sinners. Divine grace and mercy are inherent in all the words of this verse: 1. The Messenger of Allah should explicitly announce the message of glad tidings. «قل» (Say) 2. Allah has addressed man directly. «یا» (O) 3. Allah has considered everyone His servant and worthy of receiving His mercy. «عبادى» (My servants) 4. The sinners have wronged themselves and have not harmed Allah. «اسرفوا على انفسهم» (who have transgressed against themselves) 5. Despair of mercy is forbidden (Haram). «لا تقنطوا» (do not despair) 6. His mercy is not limited. «رحمة اللّه» (the Mercy of Allah). (The use of «رحمة اللّه» instead of «رحمتى» [My mercy] is a symbol of the comprehensiveness of the mercy, as the word «اللّه» is His most comprehensive Name). 7. The promise of mercy is certain. «انّ اللّه» (Indeed, Allah) 8. Allah's action is continuous forgiveness. «یغفر» (He forgives) 9. Allah forgives all sins. «الذنوب» (the sins) 10. Allah emphasizes the forgiveness of all sins. «جمیعاً» (all) 11. Allah is the Most Forgiving and the Most Merciful. «انّه هو الغفور الرحیم» (Indeed, He is the Forgiving, the Merciful)

With all this compassion and kindness, Allah declares that the threats in the previous verses are for the education of mankind, not for vengeance or malice.

In the culture of the Quran, Israf (extravagance/transgression) has a broad meaning which, besides extravagance in wealth, also includes extravagance concerning the soul and life (nafs). This means that a person exceeds the limit of moderation in utilizing his inherent talents and capacities, falling into excess or deficiency.

The Quran's intent when it says that Allah forgives all sins is not that a person should commit sins and then say Allah will forgive, but rather the intent is that all sins, no matter how great, are forgivable, and one must not despair of Divine mercy. Naturally, the path to receiving Divine forgiveness is repentance and making amends for the sin, which is mentioned in the next verse.

Messages

  1. Divine laws and decrees are within the limits of moderation, and people's disobedience is a transgression beyond the limit of moderation. «اسرفوا على انفسهم»
  2. In Divine forgiveness, the type or amount of the sin makes no difference. «الذنوب جمیعاً»
  3. Despair of Divine mercy is not permissible. «لا تقنطوا»
  4. The forgiveness of sin is a requirement of Allah's mercy. «رحمة اللّه انّ اللّه یغفر»
  5. Hope for forgiveness is the prerequisite for absolution. «لا تقنطوا... انّ اللّه یغفر»
  6. It is Allah Himself Who forgives all errors. «انّه هو الغفور» instead of «انّه الغفور»

Ayah

وَأَنِيبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ وَأَسْلِمُوا۟ لَهُۥ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَكُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ ثُمَّ لَا تُنصَرُونَ (٥٤)
Turn penitently to Him and submit to Him before the punishment overtakes you, whereupon you will not be helped. (54)

Key Points

The preceding Ayah announced divine mercy and forgiveness to prepare the way for people's return, while this Ayah commands repentance (Tawbah) and turning back (Inabah). To articulate the necessity of repentance and return, it raises the threat of punishment.

Messages

1- Repentance is a prerequisite for benefiting from divine forgiveness. «یغفر الذنوب جمیعاً... انیبوا»

2- Repentance must be accompanied by submission (Taslim), otherwise, it is hypocrisy. «انیبوا - اسلموا»

3- Divine forgiveness is conditional upon submission and following His commands. «اسلموا»

4- We must seize opportunities, as repentance is accepted before the punishment arrives. «من قبل ان یأتیكم العذاب»

5- Other than returning to Allah, there is no path to salvation and attaining divine help. «ثمّ لا تنصرون»

Ayah
