وَٱتَّبِعُوٓا۟ أَحْسَنَ مَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُم مِّن رَّبِّكُم مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَكُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ بَغْتَةً وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَشْعُرُونَ (٥٥)
Follow the best of what has been sent down to you from your Lord, before the punishment overtakes you suddenly while you are unaware.’ (55)

Key Points

Allah has performed all His works in the best possible manner (Ahsan Wajh): He has created everything in the best way. «احسن كلّ شى ء خلقه»(54), «احسن الخالقین»(55), «فى احسن تقویم»(56) His stories are the best. «احسن القصص»(57) His judgment is the best. «و من احسن من اللّه حكما»(58) He has also asked us that our work should be done in the best manner (Ahsan). We should follow the best speech. «فیتبعون احسنه»(59) We should speak the best word. «یقولوا التى هى احسن»(60) We should respond to evil with the best method. «ادفع بالتى هى احسن»(61) We should respond to people's greetings better. «فحیّوا باحسن منها»(62) We should deal with the orphan's property in the best way. «لا تقربوا مال الیتیم الاّ بالتى هى احسن»(63) We should debate and converse with people using the best method. «جادلهم بالتى هى احسن»(64)

54) Sajdah, 7. 55) Mu'minun, 14. 56) Tin, 4. 57) Yusuf, 3. 58) Ma'idah, 50. 59) Zumar, 18. 60) Isra, 53. 61) Fussilat, 34. 62) Nisa, 84. 63) Isra, 34. 64) Nahl, 125.

Messages

1- In the method of propagation (Dawah), the philosophy behind the invitation should also be stated. (Follow the Qur'an because it is the best path and comes from the Lord.) «اتبعوا احسن... ربّكم»

2- Repentance (Inabah) and submission (Taslim) alone are not sufficient; action is necessary. «انیبوا - اسلموا - اتبعوا»

3- Since human life and capacity are limited, and the instructions leading to perfection are numerous, the selection of the best (Ahsan) must necessarily be involved. «احسن ما انزل الیكم»

4- The Qur'an is the most comprehensive and complete of Allah's revelations (Nazilat) for humanity. «احسن ما انزل...»

5- Divine instructions are a means for the growth and nurturing (Tarbiyah) of humanity. «انزل الیكم من ربّكم»

6- The remedy for an event must be applied before it occurs. (Repentance, before the punishment arrives.) «قبل ان یأتیكم العذاب»

7- Divine wrath gives no warning. «بغتةً»

8- Divine wrath is beyond human calculations. «و انتم لا تشعرون»

Ayah

أَن تَقُولَ نَفْسٌ يَـٰحَسْرَتَىٰ عَلَىٰ مَا فَرَّطتُ فِى جَنۢبِ ٱللَّهِ وَإِن كُنتُ لَمِنَ ٱلسَّـٰخِرِينَ (٥٦)
Lest anyone should say, ‘Alas for my negligence in the vicinage of Allah! Indeed I was among those who ridiculed.’ (56)

Key Points

Hasrat (حسرت) means intense regret, tafrīt (تفریط) means falling short or neglecting, and janb (جَنب) means side or direction. We read in narrations that the infallible Imams (عليهم السلام) are the Janb Allāh (Side of Allah), and in more than ten hadiths, it is stated that falling short regarding the Janb Allāh means failing to support Amīr al-Mu’minīn (عليه السلام). (65) Imam Baqir (عليه السلام) says: The most intense regret on the Day of Judgment will be for scholars who did not act upon their knowledge.

65) Tafsīr Atyab al-Bayān.

Messages

1- The Day of Judgment is the Day of Regret. «يَا حَسْرَتَى»

2- Abandoning repentance (inābah), submission, and following revelation constitutes falling short. «فَرَّطْتُ»

3- That which is beside Allah must be followed. «جَنبِ اللَّهِ» (The Book of Allah, the Awliyā’ (Friends) of Allah, the Prophet of Allah)

4- The result of mocking the truth in this world is self-abasement on the Day of Judgment. «يَا حَسْرَتَى - سَاخِرِينَ»

5- The Day of Judgment is the day of affirmation and confession. «فَرَّطْتُ - كُنْتُ لَمِنَ السَّاخِرِينَ»

6- The root of shortcomings is taking the divine commands lightly, and worse than that, mocking them. «لَمِنَ السَّاخِرِينَ»

Ayahs

أَوْ تَقُولَ لَوْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هَدَىٰنِى لَكُنتُ مِنَ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (٥۷)
Or say, ‘Had Allah guided me I would have surely been among the Godwary!’ (57)
أَوْ تَقُولَ حِينَ تَرَى ٱلْعَذَابَ لَوْ أَنَّ لِى كَرَّةً فَأَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (٥۸)
Or say, when he sights the punishment, ‘If only there were a second chance for me, I would be among the virtuous!’ (58)

Key Points

Although in Ayah 57, the criminal wants to say: Allah did not guide me, which is why I went down this path, but in Ayah 59, he hears the response that We guided you and completed the proof against you, but you yourself were prone to denial and arrogance.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the criminal seeks to exonerate himself. «لو انّ اللّه هدانى»

2- Man needs Divine guidance for salvation and deliverance. «انّ اللّه هدانى لكنت من المتّقین»

3- The sign of accepting Divine guidance is piety (taqwa) and righteousness. «لو انّ اللّه هدانى لكنت من المتّقین»

4- On the Day of Judgment, the value of piety becomes clear. «لو انّ اللّه هدانى لكنت من المتّقین»

5- On the Day of Judgment, sinners envy the state of the pious (Muttaqin). «... لكنت من المتّقین»

6- The way to prevent sin is by drawing attention to the dangerous scenes of the Day of Judgment. «اَوْ تقول... اَوْ تقول»

7- Piety (taqwa) and goodness (Ihsan) are two means of salvation. «لكنت من المتّقین... فاكون من المحسنین»

8- The wish of sinners on the Day of Judgment is to return to the world to perform good deeds (Ihsan) (for themselves and others). «لو انّ لى كرّة فاكون من المحسنین»

9- Returning to the world is impossible. «لو انّ لى كرّةً...» (The word «لَو» [if only] is used in cases where the fulfillment is impossible).

Ayah

بَلَىٰ قَدْ جَآءَتْكَ ءَايَـٰتِى فَكَذَّبْتَ بِهَا وَٱسْتَكْبَرْتَ وَكُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (٥۹)
[They will be told,] ‘Yes, My signs certainly came to you, but you denied them and acted arrogantly and you were among the faithless.’ (59)

Key Points

The criminal (sinner) will have several statements on the Day of Resurrection: 1. Confession of negligence. "فرطت فى جنب اللّه" 2. Confession of mockery. "لمن الساخرین" 3. The wish for guidance. "لو ان اللّه هدانى" 4. The wish to return to the world. "لو انّ لى كَرّة" Allah accepts his confessions, but He answers his third statement in this Ayah (that We guided you, but you yourself did not accept it), and He answers his fourth statement elsewhere (that even if they were returned to the world, they would still commit transgressions). "لو ردّوا لعادوا"(66)

66) Al-An'am, 28.

Messages

1- Allah does not punish anyone without completing the argument (proof). "بلى قد جاءتك آیاتى"

2- Immediate denial without reflection indicates a spirit of arrogance and obstinacy. "فكَذّبت"

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ تَرَى ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَبُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ وُجُوهُهُم مُّسْوَدَّةٌ ۚ أَلَيْسَ فِى جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوًى لِّلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ (٦٠)
On the Day of Resurrection you will see those who attributed lies to Allah with their faces blackened. Is not the [final] abode of the arrogant in hell? (60)

Key Points

| Lying against Allah has many examples, including: a) Associating partners with Allah. b) Likening God to something. c) Considering the angels as children of God. d) Attributing one's own evil deed to Allah. e) Claiming divinity or prophethood. f) Distortion and innovation (bid'ah) in divine laws. We read in narrations: If a person forges a hadith and attributes it to the infallible Imams علیهم السلام, he has in reality lied against Allah, because the speech of the Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام originates from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and the speech of the Prophet originates from God (Allah).(67)

67) Tafsir Burhan

Messages

1- Those who darken the luminous countenance of Allah with lies, their punishment is that their own faces will be blackened on the Day of Judgment. «كذبوا على اللّه وجوههم مسودّة»

2- Although natural blackness is not a defect (like the blackness of hair and eyes), acquired blackness is a sign of humiliation and disgrace. «وجوههم مسودّة»

3- True disgrace (blackness of face) and the blackness of the heart will become manifest on the Day of Judgment. «وجوههم مسودّة»

4- The motivation for lying against Allah is arrogance. «كذبوا على اللّه... للمتكبّرین»

Ayah

وَيُنَجِّى ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ بِمَفَازَتِهِمْ لَا يَمَسُّهُمُ ٱلسُّوٓءُ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (٦۱)
Allah will deliver those who were Godwary with their salvation. No ill shall touch them, nor will they grieve. (61)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about the disgrace of those who lie against Allah and the inhabitants of the Fire, while in this Ayah, the discussion is about the salvation of the pious (the muttaqin).

Messages

1- Piety (taqwa) is the means of salvation and prosperity. «و ینجّى اللّه الّذین اتّقوا بمفازتهم»

2- No external evil approaches the pious (the muttaqin). «لا یمسّهم السّوء»

3- The pious (the muttaqin) have no internal sorrow. «و لا هم یحزنون»

Ayahs

ٱللَّهُ خَـٰلِقُ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ وَكِيلٌ (٦۲)
Allah is creator of all things, and He watches over all things. (62)
لَّهُۥ مَقَالِيدُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰسِرُونَ (٦۳)
To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth, and those who disbelieve in the signs of Allah—it is they who are the losers. (63)

Key Points

The polytheists accepted Allah's role as Creator, just as we read in Ayah 38 of this very Surah that if you ask them, "Who is the Creator of the heavens and the earth?" they will say, "Allah." But they also attributed supervision and protection to the idols.

Messages

1- The true monotheist is one who believes in all dimensions of Tawhid (Monotheism):

Tawhid in Creation. «خالق كلّ شى ء»

Tawhid in Lordship (Rububiyyah). «على كلّ شى ء وكیل»

Tawhid in Worship. The next Ayah: «افغیر اللّه تأمرونّى اعبد...»

2- All existence is dependent on Him for creation and survival. «خالق كلّ شى ء و هو على كلّ شى ء وكیل»

3- Allah is the Creator of existence, and He can create again. «خالق... له مقالید»

4- The disbelief of the deniers does not harm Allah, because He is the Owner of the heavens and the earth. «و الّذین كفروا... اولئك هم الخاسرون»

Ayahs

قُلْ أَفَغَيْرَ ٱللَّهِ تَأْمُرُوٓنِّىٓ أَعْبُدُ أَيُّهَا ٱلْجَـٰهِلُونَ (٦٤)
Say, ‘Will you, then, bid me to worship other than Allah, O you ignorant ones?!’ (64)
وَلَقَدْ أُوحِىَ إِلَيْكَ وَإِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكَ لَئِنْ أَشْرَكْتَ لَيَحْبَطَنَّ عَمَلُكَ وَلَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (٦٥)
Certainly it has been revealed to you and to those [who have been] before you: ‘If you ascribe a partner to Allah your works shall fail and you shall surely be among the losers. (65)

Key Points

Polytheism (Shirk) after monotheism (Tawhid) nullifies acts of worship. In this Ayah, polytheism, and in Ayah 217 of Surah Al-Baqarah, apostasy (Irtidad) are introduced as causes for the nullification of deeds (Habt al-'Amal). «و من یرتدد منكم عن دینه فیمت و هو كافر فاولئك حبطت اعمالهم فى الدنیا و الآخرة» Although the primary addressee in these verses is the Prophet himself, all people share the same responsibility regarding these matters with the Prophet. Allah's address to the Prophet is due to the importance of the subject, indicating that even if the Prophet were to commit polytheism for a moment, his deeds would be ruined and he would be among the losers. Alternatively, this address is given using the idiom, "speaking to the door so the wall may hear" (meaning, addressing the Prophet to warn the community).

Messages

1- Ignorance is the source of deviations. «افغیر اللّه... اعبد ایها الجاهلون»

2- Never submit to the demands of the unwise. «تأمرونّى... ایها الجاهلون»

3- Worship must be based on the knowledge (Ma'rifah) of the Worshipped One. «افغیر اللّه اعبد... ایها الجاهلون»

4- The unwise seek to mislead others, even the Prophets. «تأمرونّى... ایها الجاهلون»

5- The Prophet of Islam is the last heavenly Prophet. «الّذین من قبلك» (The phrase «من بعدك» [after you] has never appeared in the Quran).

6- Polytheism is unforgivable for anyone. «لئن اشركتَ»

7- The infallibility ('Ismah) of the Prophets does not preclude divine command, prohibition, encouragement, or threat. «لئن اشركتَ»

8- The danger of polytheism is very serious. (The emphatic lām (lām al-maftūḥah), which signifies emphasis, is repeated three times). «لئن - لیحبطنّ - لتكوننّ»

Ayahs

بَلِ ٱللَّهَ فَٱعْبُدْ وَكُن مِّنَ ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ (٦٦)
No, worship Allah, and be among the grateful!’ (66)
وَمَا قَدَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِۦ وَٱلْأَرْضُ جَمِيعًا قَبْضَتُهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ وَٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ مَطْوِيَّـٰتٌۢ بِيَمِينِهِۦ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (٦۷)
They do not regard Allah with the regard due to Him, yet the entire earth will be in His fist on the Day of Resurrection, and the heavens, scrolled, in His right hand. Immaculate is He and exalted above [having] any partners that they ascribe [to Him]. (67)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of "Yamin" (right hand) is the hand of Allah's power. (68) The commands of Allah are of two types: 1. Mawlawi (Lordly) command, which Allah issues as the Master (Mawla), and the intellect has no access to its secrets, such as the command to perform Tawaf (circumambulation) in Mecca. «و لیطّوفوا بالبیت العتیق» (69) 2. Irshadi (Guidance) command, the secrets and codes of which the intellect also comprehends, and Allah's decree is a guidance toward the decree of the intellect, such as the phrase «كن من الشاكرین» (Be among the grateful), because the intellect dictates gratitude toward the benefactor.

(68) Tawhid Saduq, p. 161. (69) Hajj, 29.

Messages

1- Monotheism is the path to salvation from loss. «لتكونن من الخاسرین بل اللّه فاعبد»

2- The best way to thank Allah is through worship. «فاعبد و كن من الشاكرین»

3- Monotheism is a sign of correct recognition and appreciation, while polytheism arises from lack of recognition and appreciation. «و ما قدرو اللّه حقّ قدره»

4- The heavens and the earth are insignificant before Divine power. «قبضته - مطویّات بیمینه»

5- To know Allah better, we must pay attention to His absolute power and encompassment. «و ما قدروا اللّه حقّ قدره و الارض جمیعاً... و السموات...»

6- We should not turn to others, for everything is solely in the hand of Allah. «و الارض... و السموات مطویّات... سبحانه و تعالى عمّا یشركون»

Ayah

وَنُفِخَ فِى ٱلصُّورِ فَصَعِقَ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۖ ثُمَّ نُفِخَ فِيهِ أُخْرَىٰ فَإِذَا هُمْ قِيَامٌ يَنظُرُونَ (٦۸)
And the Trumpet will be blown, and whoever is in the heavens will swoon and whoever is on the earth, except whomever Allah wishes. Then it will be blown a second time, behold, they will rise up, looking on! (68)

Key Points

According to the explicit text of this Ayah, the end of the life of this world (dunya) and the beginning of the Resurrection (Qiyamah) occur through the blowing (of the Trumpet/Horn, nafkhah). This blowing is sometimes referred to as the sounding of the Horn (Sūr) or Trumpet: «نُقِر فى النّاقور»(70), sometimes by the term «القارعة»(71) meaning the Striker/Calamity, sometimes by the term «الصّاخة»(72) meaning the terrifying sound, and sometimes by the term «صیحة»(73) (the Shout). The disruption of the cosmic order and the destruction of nature through the creation of a terrifying sound is believable for those who have heard the sound of an explosion wave or the breaking of the sound barrier on the battlefield. Narrations (Riwāyāt) identify the angel responsible for blowing the Horn as Isrāfīl.(74) All human beings will die at the time of the blowing of the Horn (nafkhah al-Sūr). Therefore, the purpose of the exception in the phrase «الاّ مَن شاء اللّه» (except whom Allah wills) is to demonstrate Divine power. Meaning, the Hand of Allah is not constrained, and at that time when everyone dies, if Allah wills, He will keep certain individuals alive. Just as in some narrations, the names of Jibrā’īl (Gabriel), Isrāfīl, Mīkā’īl (Michael), or the martyrs (shuhadā’) are mentioned as those who remain alive.(75)

70) Al-Muddaththir, 8. 71) Al-Qāri‘ah, 1. 72) ‘Abasa, 23. 73) Yā Sīn, 53. 74) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 75) Tafāsīr Al-Mīzān, Nūr al-Thaqalayn, and Durr al-Manthūr.

Messages

  1. The death and resurrection of all human beings are easy for Allah, like the blowing of a trumpet. «نُفخ - صَعق»

  2. Know that the occurrence of the Resurrection (Qiyamah) is certain. (Although Qiyamah will occur in the future, the verbs related to it are used in the past tense, which is a sign of its certainty.) «نفخ - صعق - ینفخ»

  3. The Resurrection is not limited only to human beings and the inhabitants of the earth. «مَن فى السموات و مَن فى الارض»

  4. There is a long period between the first and second blowing (nafkhah) of the Resurrection. «نفخ... ثُمّ نفخ»

  5. The Resurrection is sudden and instantaneous. «فاذا هم» is a sign of suddenness.

Ayah

وَأَشْرَقَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ بِنُورِ رَبِّهَا وَوُضِعَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ وَجِا۟ىٓءَ بِٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ وَٱلشُّهَدَآءِ وَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (٦۹)
The earth will glow with the light of her Lord, and the Book will be set up, and the prophets and the martyrs will be brought, and judgment will be made between them with justice, and they will not be wronged. (69)

Key Points

The meaning of nūr Rabb (the Light of the Lord) is either the light of truth and justice, with which Allah illuminates the earth on that Day, or it refers to a light other than the light of the sun and the moon, which Allah creates on that Day. Of course, the late Allamah Tabataba'i states in Tafsir al-Mizan that the meaning of nūr Rabb is the unveiling of secrets and realities on the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). Then, to support his view, He cites Ayah 22 of Surah Qaf: «لقد كنت فى غفلة من هذا فكشفنا عنك غطاءك» (Indeed, you were in neglect of this; now We have removed from you your cover). We read in Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan: Since the light of the sun and the moon will be extinguished on the Day of Judgment, the meaning of nūr Rabb is the light that emanates from the believers. Then He mentions this Ayah as evidence: «یوم ترى المؤمنین و المؤمنات یسعى نورهم بین ایدیهم»(76)

(76) Al-Hadid, 13; At-Tahrim, 8.

Messages

1- The presentation of the record of deeds, the summoning of the prophets and witnesses, and the judgment based on truth on the Day of Judgment are certain matters. (The words «قُضِىَ» [it was judged], «وُضِعَ» [it was placed], and «جاى ءَ» [they were brought] are presented in the passive past tense form).

2- There will be multiple witnesses involved on the Day of Judgment. (Prophets, Imams, angels, body parts, the earth, time, etc.). «الكتاب - النبیّین - الشهداء»

3- All human behavior and actions in this world are under the supervision of a group of observers and witnesses, because the condition for bearing witness on the Day of Judgment is presence and supervision over the deeds in this world. «و جیى ءَ بالنبیّین و الشهداء»

4- Although the Prophets will also be questioned on the Day of Judgment, «و لنسئلن المرسلین»(77) (And We will surely question the Messengers), but the summoning of the Prophets in this Ayah, because they are placed alongside the Book and the witnesses, is for the purpose of testifying to the deeds of their own nations. «و جیى ءَ بالنبیّین و الشهداء»

5- Allah's judgment and verdict are based on the documentation of the record of deeds and the testimony of the Prophets and the witnesses. «وضع الكتاب وجیى ء بالنبیّین و الشهداء و قُضِىَ»

(77) Al-A'raf, 6.

Ayahs

وَوُفِّيَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا عَمِلَتْ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَفْعَلُونَ (۷٠)
Every soul will be recompensed fully for what it has done, and He is best aware of what they do. (70)
وَسِيقَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ زُمَرًا ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءُوهَا فُتِحَتْ أَبْوَٰبُهَا وَقَالَ لَهُمْ خَزَنَتُهَآ أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِّنكُمْ يَتْلُونَ عَلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّكُمْ وَيُنذِرُونَكُمْ لِقَآءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَـٰذَا ۚ قَالُوا۟ بَلَىٰ وَلَـٰكِنْ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَةُ ٱلْعَذَابِ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۷۱)
The faithless will be driven to hell in throngs. When they reach it and its gates are opened, its keepers will say to them, ‘Did there not come to you [any] apostles from among yourselves, reciting to you the signs of your Lord and warning you of the encounter of this day of yours?’ They will say, ‘Yes, but the word of punishment became due against the faithless.’ (71)
قِيلَ ٱدْخُلُوٓا۟ أَبْوَٰبَ جَهَنَّمَ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ فَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى ٱلْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ (۷۲)
It will be said, ‘Enter the gates of hell to remain in it [forever]. Evil is the [ultimate] abode of the arrogant.’ (72)

Key Points

Sayq (سیق) is derived from sūq (سوق), meaning to drive forward. The market is called sūq because the attractions of the market drive (or draw) a person toward it. The word Zumar (زُمر) means community or group, and the naming of this Surah is based on this Ayah (71) and Ayah 73. (78) The angels drag the inhabitants of Hell toward Hell. «وجاءت كلّ نفس معها سائق وشهید» (79)

(78) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. (79) Qaf, 21.

Messages

1- There is no deficiency or shortage in divine punishment and reward. (Wuffiyat (وفّیت) means complete fulfillment.)

2- On the Day of Judgment, no one is exempt from punishment or reward. «كل نفسٍ»

3- Allah knows better than anyone else; summoning witnesses and evidence is for establishing the court system, not for Allah to acquire knowledge. «اعلم بما یفعلون»

4- Dragging the criminals toward Hell is a humiliation separate from other punishments. «سیق الّذین كفروا»

5- The gates of Hell are closed, and as soon as the inhabitants of Hell reach it, they suddenly open, which is terrifying in itself. «جاؤوها فتحت ابوابها»

6- Hell has multiple gates. «ابوابها» (We also read in Ayah 44 of Surah Al-Hijr: «لها سبعة ابواب»)

7- On the Day of Judgment, the angels will have direct conversation with the inhabitants of Hell. «قال لهم خزنتها»

8- Hell has specific custodians (or guards). «خزنتها»

9- The argument has been completed against the criminals (they have been warned sufficiently). «یأتكم رسل یتلون علیكم آیات ربّكم»

10- In addition to reciting the divine signs, the prophets warned the people. «یتلون... ینذرونكم»

11- The Day of Judgment is the day of confession and acknowledgment. «قالوا بلى »

12- The reason the punishment is realized is the disbelief (kufr) of the people. «حقّت... على الكافرین»

13- The root of disbelief is arrogance. «الكافرین... المتكبّرین»

Ayah

وَسِيقَ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ رَبَّهُمْ إِلَى ٱلْجَنَّةِ زُمَرًا ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءُوهَا وَفُتِحَتْ أَبْوَٰبُهَا وَقَالَ لَهُمْ خَزَنَتُهَا سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَيْكُمْ طِبْتُمْ فَٱدْخُلُوهَا خَـٰلِدِينَ (۷۳)
Those who are wary of their Lord will be led to paradise in throngs. When they reach it and its gates are opened, its keepers will say to them, ‘Peace be to you! You are welcome! Enter it to remain [forever].’ (73)

Key Points

In Ayah 71, we read: As soon as the inhabitants of Hell reach Hell, the doors are opened for them, «جاؤها فتحت». But in this Ayah, He says: «و فتحت», meaning the inhabitants of Paradise reach Paradise while the doors have already been opened for them. It is narrated in the book Khisal from Imam Ali عليه السلام that Paradise has eight doors, and each door is specific to a group. One door is for the Prophets and the truthful (Siddiqeen), one for the martyrs and the righteous (Salihun), five doors for My Shi'a (followers), and one door for the Muslims who do not bear hatred towards Me.(80)

Question: Although the lifespan and the good and bad deeds of man in this world are limited, why is the punishment and reward in the Hereafter unlimited, such that the Quran states that the inhabitants of Hell and Paradise will abide eternally in the Fire or in Paradise?

Answer: Firstly, eternity in Paradise is due to Allah's grace (lutf) and does not contradict justice. Secondly, eternity in Hell is not for all inhabitants of Hell; rather, many criminals will enter Paradise after receiving the punishment for their deeds and being purified from sin. Thirdly, the duration of the punishment depends on the severity or lightness of the crime, not the time taken to commit the crime. It is possible that someone attempts to murder another person in an instant, and even fails, yet the court sentences him to life imprisonment. Is the punishment for striking a match near a barrel of gasoline the same as striking one near a tanker ship full of gasoline? Yes, the one who disobeyed the Creator of existence and the Great God throughout his entire life will remain in torment forever.

80) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- The path to entering Paradise is purity and piety. «سیق الّذین اتّقوا ربّهم الى الجنّة»

2- Paradise also has multiple doors. «ابوابها»

3- «سلام علیكم» is the greeting of the angels to the inhabitants of Paradise, and this type of greeting is also recommended in this world.

4- The condition for entering Paradise is purity, which is either present from the beginning or acquired through repentance. «طبتم فادخلوها»

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى صَدَقَنَا وَعْدَهُۥ وَأَوْرَثَنَا ٱلْأَرْضَ نَتَبَوَّأُ مِنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ حَيْثُ نَشَآءُ ۖ فَنِعْمَ أَجْرُ ٱلْعَـٰمِلِينَ (۷٤)
They will say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, who has fulfilled His promise to us and made us inheritors of the earth, that we may settle in paradise wherever we may wish!’ How excellent is the reward of the workers [of righteousness]! (74)

Key Points

The meaning of al-ard (the earth/land) in this Ayah is the land of Paradise (Heaven).

Messages

1- Saying al-hamdulillah (Praise be to Allah) after receiving a blessing is the practice of the people of Paradise. «قالوا الحمدللّه»

2- The right to choose one's dwelling is one of the blessings and privileges of Paradise. «نتبوّء من الجنّة حیث نشاء»

3- Everyone can attain the divine rewards through good deeds. «اجر العاملین»

4- Paradise is granted for a price (effort/value), not for an excuse. «اجر العاملین»

Ayah

وَتَرَى ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ حَآفِّينَ مِنْ حَوْلِ ٱلْعَرْشِ يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ ۖ وَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ وَقِيلَ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۷٥)
And you will see the angels surrounding the Throne, celebrating the praise of their Lord, and judgment will be made between them with justice, and it will be said, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds!’ (75)

Key Points

"حافّین" (Hāffīn) is derived from haff, meaning surrounding or encompassing, and the ‘Arsh (Throne) is the base of Divine power and will.

Messages

1- Angels are visible to the friends (Awliyā’) of Allah. «ترى الملائكة»

2- The Throne (‘Arsh) is the station of the angels. «حافّین من حول العرش»

3- The angels are ready to execute the Divine commands. «حافّین من حول العرش»

4- The angels are constantly engaged in glorification (Tasbīh). «یسبّحون»

5- Praise (Hamd) and glorification (Tasbīh) are coupled together. «یسبّحون بحمد ربّهم»

6- Angels, like humans, are under the nurturing care of their Lord. «ربّهم»

7- Allah’s judgment on the Day of Resurrection is based on truth (Haqq) and justice (‘Adl). «قضى بینهم بالحقّ» (We must act based on truth in this world, because we will be judged by the truth.) «قُضى بینهم بالحقّ»

8- All praise and adoration are due to Him Who nurtures the entire existence.
